schemeName: Additional Pension to 100% Disabled Ex-Serviceman having Children
briefDescription: The scheme provides an additional pension to 100% disabled ex-servicemen who were members of the Armed Forces & have been released from service due to 100% disability or permanent incapacity to work, provided the disability or incapacity is attributable to service.

schemeName: Additional Pension to Widow of Ex-Serviceman having Children
briefDescription: The scheme is implemented by the Sainik and Ardh Sainik Welfare Department, Government of Haryana. Under the scheme, an additional amount, in addition to the pension, is provided to the widow of an ex-serviceman who dies while in service but not in any battle.

schemeName: Sanction of Adult Sticks to Senior Citizens
briefDescription: The "Sanction of Adult Sticks to Senior Citizens" scheme aims to provide walking sticks to senior citizens. This initiative ensures that eligible senior citizens receive the necessary support to enhance their mobility and independence.

schemeName: Marriage Grant for the Marriage of the Daughter of War Disabled Ex-Servicemen above 50% Disability
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹21,000/- is provided for the marriage of each dependent daughter of war-disabled ex-servicemen whose disability has been assessed above 50%, for up to two daughters who are at least 18 years old.

schemeName: Marriage Grant to War Widows’ Daughters of Ex-Servicemen
briefDescription: The scheme is implemented by the Sainik and Ardh Sainik Welfare Department, Government of Haryana. Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹21,000/- is provided to the war widows for marriage of their dependent daughters, for up to two daughters who are at least 18 years old.

schemeName: Scheme for Celebration of Birth/death Anniversary of Eminent Personalities
briefDescription: The "Scheme for Celebration of Birth/Death Anniversary of Eminent Personalities" scheme aims to provide financial assistance for the celebration of anniversaries/Jayanti's of Sant Kabir, Sant Gadge, and Sant Durbalnath and in addition to Anniversaries of DR. B.R. Ambedkar, etc.

schemeName: IMA Nongthangleima Yaipha Tengbang Scheme
briefDescription: The "Ima Nongthangleima Yaipha Tengbang Scheme" provides support to approximately 4 lakh women in Manipur who are above the age of 40 years. The scheme aims to empower these women by offering crucial financial assistance, thereby aiding them in pursuing diverse livelihood options.

schemeName: Bharati Scheme for Education Professional Education Course - Chartered Accountancy Intern
briefDescription: The "Bharati Scheme for Education Professional Education Course - Chartered Accountancy Intern (IPCC)" encourages Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education. Under this scheme, financial assistance for the academic year will be granted to eligible poor Brahmin students.

schemeName: Bharati Scheme for Education - Graduation
briefDescription: The "Bharati Scheme for Education - Graduation (Any regular course of 3 years duration after 10+2 will be considered under this category. i.e., B.A., B.Com, B.Sc., etc., including B.Ed.)" provides financial assistance to encourage Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education.

schemeName: Financial Assistance to Disabled Ex-Servicemen
briefDescription: Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹3,000/- per month shall be granted only to those Disabled Ex-Servicemen who are bonafide residents of Haryana State, who were discharged from the Armed Forces on medical grounds, and whose disability is not attributable to service.

schemeName: Kanya Saksharta Protsahan Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme was launched by the Tribal Welfare Department, Govt. of Madhya Pradesh. The objective of the scheme is to motivate girls towards education. Under this scheme, girls in Class 11th who have passed their 10th class examination will receive ₹3,000/- per year as a monetary incentive.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Anusuchit Jati Evam Anusuchit Janjati Chhatrawas Anudan Yojana
briefDescription: The “Mukhyamantri Anusoochit Jaati Evam Anusoochit Janajati Chhatrawas Anudan Yojana” was launched by the SC and ST Welfare Department, Government of Bihar aims to provide financial assistance in every month to the students studying in Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe hostels.

schemeName: Financial Assistance to War Widows of Defence Forces
briefDescription: Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹3,000/- per month shall be granted to War Widows of Defence Force Personnel’s (Army, Navy & Air Force) who were killed in action during declared war of 1962, 1965 & 1971 in addition to family pension already getting from Government of India.

schemeName: Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Meritorious Students Incentive Scheme (Class 12th Passed)
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to motivate students from the lower economic backgrounds & support them economically. Under this scheme, cash award of ₹5,000/- is given to the students who have obtained the highest marks in the 12th class examination in government schools within their district.

schemeName: Hostel Facility for Students Availing Education in Delhi
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to provide facilities for residential and other facilities such as water, reimbursement of electricity expenditure, scholarship etc. for scheduled caste students of Madhya Pradesh studying in higher institutions located in Delhi.

schemeName: State Marriage Assistance Scheme
briefDescription: The 'State Marriage Assistance Scheme' offers one-time financial aid to female beneficiaries from families holding AAY or PHH ration cards, with the amount directly credited to their Aadhaar-linked bank accounts. Additionally, divyangjan beneficiaries also receive financial assistance.

schemeName: State Government ST Scholarship (Class 9 to 10)
briefDescription: The scheme was launched by the Tribal Affairs Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, aims to encourage education among Scheduled Tribe students and improve their educational status. This scheme provides scholarships to Scheduled Tribe students in Classes 9 and 10.

schemeName: Pratibha Yojana
briefDescription: This scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Tribe students who have passed national competitive examinations such as JEE, NEET, CLAT, AIIMS, and NDA, and have taken admission in IIT, NIT, NLU, MBBS (government colleges located in Madhya Pradesh), AIIMS or NDA.

schemeName: Medical Expenses-Treatment for Major Ailments Including Surgery
briefDescription: "Medical Expenses-Treatment for Major Ailments Including Surgery'' scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Dept.,West Bengal, is a welfare scheme that provides medical expenses for the treatment of beneficiary or his/ her dependents suffering from Cancer, Heart disease, Kidney disease, T.B. etc.

schemeName: Niji Talabon Ka Jirnoddhar Ki Yojana
briefDescription: The government of Bihar launched the "Niji Talabon Ka Jirnoddhar Ki Yojana" scheme aims to enhance the water holding capacity of private ponds to boost fish productivity & production as well as provide benefits to private fish farmers through the expansion/renovation of their ponds.

schemeName: Integrated Social Security Scheme: Pension for Women in Distress
briefDescription: The scheme is implemented by the Social and Tribal Welfare Department, Administration of Union Territory of Ladakh. Under this scheme, a financial assistance of ₹1,000/- per month is provided as a pension to destitute widows, divorced, or deserted women with no source of income.

schemeName: Assistance in Case of Accidents (O.B.O.C.W.W.B)
briefDescription: The “Assistance in Case of Accidents” scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Odisha, is a welfare scheme that provides financial assistance to the beneficiary in case of hospitalization and becoming disabled due to an accident.

schemeName: Indira Gandhi Pyari Behna Sukh-Samman Nidhi Yojana
briefDescription: The "Indira Gandhi Pyari Behna Sukh-Samman Nidhi Yojana" is an initiative by the Himachal Pradesh government. The scheme recognizes the significant contributions of women in household work, cultural preservation, and family economy by providing a monthly assistance.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: The NSFDC Internship Scheme
briefDescription: "The NSFDC Internship Scheme 2024" is implemented by the National Scheduled Castes Finance & Development Corporation, Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Govt. of India. The internship will expose students to various programs & schemes undertaken by NSFDC, offering them hands-on experience in

schemeName: Scheme for Funeral Assistance in Case of Death of Registered Construction Worker
briefDescription: The "Scheme for Funeral Assistance in Case of Death of Registered Construction Worker" was launched by the Tripura Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department Tripura. The scheme aims to provide funeral assistance in case of the death of registered construction workers.

schemeName: Research Scholarship for Disabled Students
briefDescription: The scheme is implemented by the Higher Education Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, aims to provide financial support for PhD research to differently-abled students. Eligible candidates receive ₹16,000/- per month, which is directly transferred to their bank accounts.

schemeName: Sanskrit Scholarship
briefDescription: Under this scheme, students studying Sanskrit at various levels—Post Graduate (M.A. Classic), Acharya, Graduate (B.A. Classic), Shastri, Uttar Madhyama, Purva Madhyama, and Prathama—in any government or aided non-government college receive a monthly scholarship of ₹300/- for 10 months.

schemeName: Hospitalisation Relief Scheme
briefDescription: This scheme was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Department Telangana financial assistance is provided to construction workers who suffer temporary disabilities due to accidents or diseases & hospitalized for five or more days.

schemeName: Tirth Barat Yojana
briefDescription: The objective of this scheme is to provide government assistance for pilgrimage to the senior citizens & Persons with Disabilities (PwDs), who are residents of Chhattisgarh, to visit one or more of the various designated pilgrimage sites outside the state, once in their lifetime.

schemeName: Maternity Benefit Scheme - Telangana
briefDescription: The "Maternity Benefit Scheme" was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Department, Telangana. The scheme provides financial assistance to registered women workers, as well as the wives & daughters of registered workers to support them.

schemeName: Marriage Gift Scheme - Telangana
briefDescription: The "Marriage Gift Scheme" was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Dept. Telangana. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance as a marriage gift to unmarried registered women workers & the daughters of registered building & construction

schemeName: Skill Upgradation and Vocational Education Scheme: For Worker (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
briefDescription: "Skill Upgradation and Vocational Education Scheme: For Worker'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Dept. of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides full course fees, boarding and lodging assistance to the registered workers who acquire Skill from technical institutions.

schemeName: Artificial Limbs and Assistive Devices Supply Scheme
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to increase the mobility of persons with disabilities by reducing impact of their disabilities. The scheme provides artificial limbs/assistive devices worth up to ₹6,900/- to persons with disabilities of 40% or more, provided they meet the other eligibility criteria.

schemeName: Potency Development Plan
briefDescription: The objective of this scheme is to increase the mobility of all eligible PwDs of the state by providing them artificial limbs/assistive devices. The scheme provides artificial limbs/assistive devices worth up to ₹6,900/- to PwDs of 40% or more, provided they meet the other eligibility criteria.

schemeName: Pension Scheme (K.B.O.C.W.W.B)
briefDescription: The "Pension Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme that aims to provide a monthly pension to registered construction workers who have reached 60 years of age and is a beneficiary of the Board for at least 3 years.

schemeName: Distribution of Certified Seeds-Oil Seeds
briefDescription: The "Distribution of Certified Seeds-Oil Seeds" scheme offers subsidies to farmers for purchasing certified oil seeds, helping them improve their crop yield and quality.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Shramshakti Yojana
briefDescription: This scheme aims to provide employment opportunities to men and women aged 18 to 45 from the minority community by offering vocational skill training in various fields or by providing loans through the Bihar State Minority Financial Corporation, Patna, for self-employment.

schemeName: Distribution of Rhizobium
briefDescription: The “Distribution of Rhizobium” scheme offers a subsidy on the cost of Rhizobium, to farmers raising seed farms with Pulses crops in Tamil Nadu.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Education of Children of Beneficiary
briefDescription: The "Financial Assistance for Education of Children of Beneficiary" welfare scheme launched by the Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board provides one-time financial assistance for the education of children.

schemeName: Animal Husbandry
briefDescription: This scheme provides subsidies on the distribution of sheep units, milch animals, and calves, as well as health coverage and artificial insemination for the animals. It is targeted at Tribal communities to improve their livelihood through animal husbandry.

schemeName: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department-Hostels: Special Guides
briefDescription: The scheme provides hostel facilities to all boys and girls boarders studying up to XII Standard.

schemeName: Charmashilpa Scheme
briefDescription: The "Charmashilpa Scheme" aims to modernize the leather artisans' production methods by offering financial assistance to setup mechanized production units. This support helps leather artisan SHGs and cooperative societies enhance the quality of their products, making them more competitive.

schemeName: Vocational Training to the Dependents
briefDescription: The "Vocational Training to the Dependents" provides a residential skill training program for 15,000 dependents of registered construction workers. The program focuses on enhancing the employability of dependents through training in various trades.

schemeName: Afforestation Schemes Providing Incentives And Employment To Tribals In Forest
briefDescription: This scheme offers grants to unemployed tribals for afforestation work, providing them with incentives and employment opportunities in forest operations.

schemeName: Assistance for Funeral Rites
briefDescription: The “Assistance for Funeral Rites” scheme provides ₹500/- to support SC/ST families with low income, in covering funeral expenses, ensuring that they can conduct funeral rites for their deceased ones.

schemeName: Loans and Advances for Construction of a House
briefDescription: The "Loans and Advances for Construction of a House" scheme by the Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board provides financial support to registered construction workers to build their own houses. Beneficiaries can avail loans based on their skill level, with amounts ranging.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Marriage (BBOCWWB)
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹50,000/- is provided to registered male or female workers who have been members of the Board for at least three years, for the marriage of their two adult daughters and for the female member's own marriage.

schemeName: Organizing Block Demonstration on Polythene Mulch Technology in Groundnut
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance to farmers for adopting polythene mulch technology in groundnut cultivation to improve productivity.

schemeName: Production Of Certified Seeds
briefDescription: The "Production of Certified Seeds" scheme, offers a subsidy to farmers for producing certified seeds of Pulses.

schemeName: District Central Cooperative Banks and through its Branches: For Consumer Durables T.V., Fridge and Two Wheelers Loan
briefDescription: A loan scheme provided by District Central Cooperative Banks for purchasing consumer durables like T.V., fridges, and two-wheelers. Loan covers 75% of the product cost at an interest rate of 14%.

schemeName: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (Maharashtra)
briefDescription: "Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (Maharashtra)" is a scheme by the Social Justice & Special Assistance Dept, Govt of Maharashtra. In this scheme, widows aged 40 to 79 years who are from a BPL family receive a pension of ₹1500/- per month.

schemeName: Children Education Allowance
briefDescription: The scheme is implemented by the Nagaland Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board, Under this scheme, children of registered beneficiaries studying from Nursery to Class 10 are provided ₹500/- per month per child as education assistance.

schemeName: District Central Cooperative Banks and through its Branches: For Housing, House Construction, Repair Extension Loan
briefDescription: The scheme offers loans up to ₹10,00,000/- for housing, house construction, and repair/extension purposes, at a 12% interest rate to provide financial aid to citizens for housing needs.

schemeName: Schooling Education (JKBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme "Schooling Education" by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Dept., J&K, provides financial assistance for the education of children of registered construction workers, from Class Nursery/1st to 5th, and from Class 6th to 8th.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Medical (JKBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The "Financial Assistance for Medical" scheme by the JKBOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu & Kashmir, provides financial support for medical expenses, including injury treatment, temporary and permanent disability, and the treatment of chronic diseases.

schemeName: District Central Cooperative Banks and through its Branches: For Women Entrepreneurs
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide loans of up to ₹5,00,000/- at an interest rate of 12% (subject to change) for women entrepreneurs through District Central Cooperative Banks and its branches.

schemeName: Mahila Samridhi Yojana (Maharashtra)
briefDescription: "Mahila Samridhi Yojana" is administered by the Dept. of Social Justice & Special Assistance, Govt. of Maharashtra. The scheme aims to uplift economically weaker women from the Charmakar Community. Loans ranging from ₹25,000/- to ₹50,000/- are granted at a low interest rate of 4% p.a.

schemeName: Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme offers financial assistance to students from Backward Classes (BC) and Most Backward Classes (MBC) studying in English medium from 6th to 10th standard. It covers tuition and examination fees.

schemeName: Marriage Assistance (JKBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The "Marriage Assistance" scheme by the JKBOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu & Kashmir, provides financial aid to registered construction workers for the marriage of their dependents, including up to three marriages for their sons, daughters, siblings, or themselves.

schemeName: Educational Assistance to the Children Studying in 1st to 7th and 8th to 10th Class
briefDescription: Under this scheme, educational assistance is provided of ₹2,500/- to the children studying in 1st to 7th standard and ₹5,000/- for the children studying in 8th to 10th standard of the registered construction worker. The scheme covers up to two children and the wife of a registered male worker.

schemeName: Mahila Kisan Yojana (Maharashtra)
briefDescription: "Mahila Kisan Yojana" is implemented by the Dept. of Social Justice & Special Assistance, Govt. of Maharashtra. The scheme aims to uplift the lifestyle of economically disadvantaged Charmakar communities. it supports the production and sale of various types of footwear and leather articles.

schemeName: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (Maharashtra)
briefDescription: The scheme "Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension" is a social welfare scheme by the Social Justice & Special Assistance Department, Government of Maharashtra. In this scheme, ₹600 per month is provided to elderly individuals aged 65 years and above from BPL families.

schemeName: Construction of Free Houses for Tribals
briefDescription: This scheme provides free houses to tribals who already possess free house site pattas. The construction of the houses is carried out by government authorities, ensuring shelter for tribal communities.

schemeName: State Disability Pension
briefDescription: The Social Welfare & Tribal Affairs Department, Government of Mizoram launched the State Disability Pension scheme. It provides a monthly pension of ₹350 to 200 eligible individuals who are completely blind or bedridden.

schemeName: Handicapped Students Stipend Mizoram
briefDescription: Under this scheme, provide financial support to differently-abled students for purchasing essential items like books and uniforms. Students enrolled in both government and private schools are eligible to receive a monthly stipend.

schemeName: Savitribai Phule Self-Help Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial support to unemployed women from the Scheduled Caste (SC) category in the state to help them start businesses in industry, service, or trade sectors.

schemeName: State Scholarship of Class 1st to 5th (Scheduled Caste)
briefDescription: Launched in 1966-67, the "State Scholarship of Class 1st to 5th" scheme is a welfare initiative by the Scheduled Caste Welfare Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme provides a scholarship amount of ₹200/- to Scheduled Caste students, which is disbursed until 31st October every year.

schemeName: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Assistance on Death and Permanent Disablement
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial relief to the beneficiaries in case of permanent disability due to accidents or their dependents/nominees in case of accidental or natural death.

schemeName: Kanya Dhan Scheme for SC Students
briefDescription: The scheme "Kanya Dhan Scheme for SC Students" by the Directorate of Higher Education, Government of Goa. Through this scheme, an amount of ₹ 25,000/- is provided to the girl students of the Scheduled Caste Communities once they pass the 12th standard board examination (or equivalent).

schemeName: Pre Matric Scholarship Scheme (Saral Vidya Sahay) for SC and OBC (V to VIII)
briefDescription: "Pre Matric Scholarship Scheme (Saral Vidya Sahay) for SC and OBC (V to VIII)" was introduced by the Directorate of Social Welfare, Government of Goa. The scheme aims to support parents of children belonging to Scheduled Castes and Other Backward Classes for the education of their wards.

schemeName: Indira Gandhi National Disabled Pension Scheme (Madhya Pradesh)
briefDescription: Launched on 1st April 2009, the scheme "Indira Gandhi National Disabled Pension Scheme" is a pension scheme by the Department of Social Welfare, Govt of Madhya Pradesh. In this scheme, a pension of ₹600/- is provided to differently-abled individuals from 'Below Poverty Line'.

schemeName: Savitribai Phule Kishori Samriddhi Yojana
briefDescription: The Savitribai Phule Kishori Samriddhi Yojana was initiated by the "Department of Women, Child Development, and Social Security, Government of Jharkhand."

schemeName: Large Sized Multi Purpose Coop. Society For Interest Free Loan
briefDescription: The scheme provides interest-free loans to citizens through a cooperative society. The maximum loan amount offered is ₹45,000/-, with zero interest charged.

schemeName: Mukhymantri Sarthi Yojana - Jharkhand
briefDescription: The "Mukhymantri Sarthi Yojana" aims to provide industry-relevant skill development training for youth in Jharkhand, aligning with NSQF standards. Implemented by Jharkhand Skill Development Mission Society (JSDMS) across all 24 districts, it offers free training to enhance employability.

schemeName: Ved-Vyas Housing Construction Scheme
briefDescription: The "Ved Vyas Housing Construction Scheme" provides financial assistance to active and traditional fish farmers in Simdega district for building houses. The scheme prioritizes individuals particularly those living in kutcha or thatched houses.

schemeName: Scheme For Loan And Advances to the Beneficiary For Construction Of House
briefDescription: The Scheme for Loan and Advances to the Beneficiary for Construction of House was launched by the Tripura Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, under the Labour Department of Tripura.

schemeName: Aawaas Sahayata
briefDescription: Launched in 2016, the "Aawaas Sahayata" scheme by the Department of Tribal Affairs, Madhya Pradesh, The scheme provides housing assistance to tribal students pursuing higher education outside their hometowns where hostels are unavailable.

schemeName: Block Level Institute for Rural Skill Acquisition (BIRSA)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide free, localized skill training to candidates at the block level in Jharkhand. The program is non-residential and operates in 24 districts across 80 blocks in the first phase.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Bagwani Mission: Dragon Fruit Vikas Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to expand dragon fruit cultivation in 21 districts of the state, providing financial assistance to ryot farmers for establishing and maintaining dragon fruit plantations.

schemeName: Sarvajan Pension Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance of per month to elderly individuals, widows, destitute women, differently-abled persons, primitive tribes, and HIV/AIDS patients. The scheme eliminates previous age restrictions and targets inclusivity by guaranteeing pensions to all eligible.

schemeName: Krishi Input Anudan Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide agricultural input subsidies in affected areas due to the rise in water levels of rivers like Ganga and Kosi and flood-related crop damage from heavy rainfall.

schemeName: Bihar Shatabdi Asangathit Karyakshetra Kamagaar Evan Shilpakar Samajik Suraksha Yojana: Total Permanent Disability
briefDescription: “Total Permanent Disability” under the “Bihar Shatabdi Asangathit Karyakshetra Kamagaar Evan Shilpakar Samajik Suraksha Yojana” launched by the Labour Resources Department, Bihar, provides financial assistance of ₹75,000/- to the families of workers or craftsmen in case of permanent disablement.

schemeName: Makhana Vikas Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to boost Makhana cultivation by providing training to farmers and financial support for traditional equipment.

schemeName: Atal Graha Jyoti Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme provides subsidized electricity bills to domestic consumers with monthly consumption up to 150 units, promoting energy conservation and reducing electricity costs for eligible beneficiaries.

schemeName: Pay & Play Scheme
briefDescription: The “Pay & Play Scheme” aims to provide state-level players with training and practice opportunities at the Mega Sports Complex, Hotwar, Ranchi.

schemeName: Jharkhand Sports Excellence Cash Award Scheme to Players
briefDescription: The “Jharkhand Sports Excellence Cash Award Scheme to Players” aims to provide cash awards to athletes for exceptional achievements in national and international competitions, as per the Jharkhand Sports Policy, 2022.

schemeName: Jharkhand State Backward Classes Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme
briefDescription: The "Jharkhand State Backward Classes Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme" aims to enhance educational opportunities and participation among students belonging to backward classes in Jharkhand. It provides financial assistance to reduce dropout rates from classes 1 to 10 and promote equality in education.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Alpasankhyak Udyami Yojana
briefDescription: This is a flagship scheme of the Bihar government aimed at promoting entrepreneurship and self-employment among the youth by providing financial assistance of up to ₹10,00,000/- for setting up businesses.

schemeName: Marang Gomke Jaipal Singh Munda Overseas Scholarship Scheme
briefDescription: The "Marang Gomke Jaipal Singh Munda Overseas Scholarship Scheme" provides financial assistance to deserving Scheduled Tribes, Scheduled Castes, Minorities, and Backward Class students for pursuing postgraduate education in top-ranked universities abroad.

schemeName: Jharkhand State Scheduled Caste Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme
briefDescription: The "Jharkhand State Scheduled Caste Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme" promotes higher education among Scheduled Caste students in Jharkhand by providing financial assistance. This scheme aims to increase the participation of SC students in post-matric education.

schemeName: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Backward Class Students
briefDescription: The scheme "Pre-Matric Scholarship for Backward Class Students" aims to provide financial assistance to backward class students studying at the pre-matriculation level in India. Monthly scholarships of varying amounts are provided to eligible students.

schemeName: Poshan Sudha
briefDescription: This scheme is a government initiative to provide nutrition and healthcare support to pregnant and lactating mothers in tribal districts of Gujarat.

schemeName: Khel Chhatravriti (Vritika) Yojana
briefDescription: The "Khel Chhatravriti (Vritika) Yojana" implemented by the Directorate of Art, Culture, Sports, and Youth Affairs, Government of Jharkhand, provides financial assistance to athletes based on their performance to encourage sports as a career.

schemeName: Ganga Swarupa Punah Lagna Arthik Sahay Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme supports remarried women who are beneficiaries of the Ganga Swarupa Pension Scheme, aiming to reintegrate them into society with financial support.

schemeName: Jharkhand State Migrant Labourers Survey and Rehabilitation Scheme
briefDescription: The "Jharkhand State Migrant Labourers Survey and Rehabilitation Scheme" aims to identify and support migrant workers from the state. The scheme ensures social security, rehabilitation, and welfare for migrant workers and their families.

schemeName: Gausevak Training (Initial and Refresher)
briefDescription: The scheme "Gausevak Training (Initial and Refresher)" aims to empower educated unemployed rural youth with self-employment opportunities and the skills to provide primary veterinary services in remote rural areas. A 100% stipend of ₹6,000 for initial training and ₹500 for refresher training.

schemeName: Civil Service Incentive Scheme (Madhya Pradesh)
briefDescription: The "Civil Service Incentive Scheme (Madhya Pradesh)" scheme by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department of the Government of Madhya Pradesh provides an incentive amount to encourage SC/ST candidates to appear in Civil Services Examinations.

schemeName: Tap Water Scheme (Nal Jal Yojana)
briefDescription: "Tap Water Scheme" (नल जल योजना) by the Panchayat and Rural Development Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh aims to provide clean water in Gram Panchayat areas in an accessible manner. The application has to be made to the concerned Gram Panchayat.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Gramin Peyjal Nishchay Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide pure drinking water through pipelines to every rural household in Bihar. This initiative started in 2016 with a focus on community involvement, local governance, and sustainable water management.

schemeName: Satat Jeevikoparjan Yojana (SJY)
briefDescription: "Satat Jeevikoparjan Yojana (SJY)" is a flagship program launched by the Government of Bihar to uplift ultra-poor households through comprehensive livelihood support, skill-building, and financial inclusion.

schemeName: Salt Distribution Scheme Jharkhand
briefDescription: The "Salt Distribution Scheme" by the Department of Food, Public Distribution and Consumer Affairs, Government of Jharkhand, ensures subsidized salt is provided to AAY, PHH, and Green Cardholders, promoting accessibility and affordability.

schemeName: Banglar Awaas Yojana (BAY)
briefDescription: "Banglar Awaas Yojana (BAY)" provides financial assistance to houseless households and those living in kutcha or dilapidated houses, helping them build pucca houses with essential amenities. The scheme offers financial assistance with additional support for toilets and unskilled workdays.

schemeName: Running of Scheduled Caste Hostel
briefDescription: The "Running of Scheduled Caste Hostels" scheme by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, aims to provide housing and a conducive study environment for Scheduled Caste students. Separate junior, senior, and college hostels are operated for boys & girls.

schemeName: Excellent Work Award Scheme for Social Worker of De-Notified Communities
briefDescription: The "Excellent Work Award Scheme for Social Workers of De-notified Communities" by the Government of Madhya Pradesh awards ₹1,00,000/- to a social worker from De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic communities for outstanding contributions to their social and economic upliftment.

schemeName: Scheme of Post Matric Scholarship to Lower Income Group Student for Studies in India
briefDescription: The scheme "Post Matric Scholarship to Lower Income Group Students for Studies in India" aims to provide financial assistance to students from the Lower Income Groups to enable them to complete their education. Students are provided maintenance allowances, tuition fees, and additional benefits.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Awas Yojna (HPBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The Mukhyamantri Awas Yojna, introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB), provides financial assistance of ₹1,50,000/- to registered worker.

schemeName: INDIRAMMA Widow Pension (Rural)
briefDescription: "INDIRAMMA Weavers Pension (Rural)" scheme aims to disburse monthly pensions to widows, irrespective of their age. To be eligible, they should be from a BPL family, be a resident of the district, and not be covered under any other Pension Scheme.

schemeName: Backward Classes Foreign Study Scholarship Scheme
briefDescription: Launched on 1st January 2007, the "Backward Classes Foreign Study Scholarship Scheme" by the Backward Classes and Minorities Welfare Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh provides financial assistance to backward class students for postgraduate, Ph.D., and post-research studies abroad.

schemeName: Empower ST/SC Communities using ICT Tools through IT Knowledge Centre (ITKC)
briefDescription: "Empower ST/SC Communities using ICT Tools through IT Knowledge Centre (ITKC)" by the Department of Information Technology, Electronics and Communication, Government of Goa aims to create a large pool of computer literate manpower and to make introducing e-governance solutions meaningful.

schemeName: Tuition Fee Reimbursement of Sainik/Public Schools
briefDescription: The "Tuition Fee Reimbursement of Sainik/Public Schools" scheme by the Department of De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Welfare, Government of Madhya Pradesh, reimburses tuition fees for eligible students enrolled in Sainik Schools or Institutions.

schemeName: INDIRAMMA Old Age Pension (Urban)
briefDescription: "INDIRAMMA Old Age Pension (Urban)" scheme aims to provide monthly pensions to destitute elderly persons aged 65 years or above with little or no means of subsistence and no family or relative to depend upon. To be eligible, they should be from a BPL family, and be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.

schemeName: Laptop Supply Scheme
briefDescription: "Laptop Supply Scheme" by the School Education Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh provides ₹25,000/- for purchasing a laptop to students who score 80% or more in 12th Standard from a Government Higher Secondary School or a Madhya Pradesh Board (MP Board) affiliated school.

schemeName: Financial Assistance to Widower and Unmarried Persons Scheme : For Unmarried
briefDescription: The aim of the scheme is to provide Social Security to Unmarried Persons who are unable to sustain themselves from their own sources and are in need of financial assistance from the State.

schemeName: Schemes for welfare of School Children belonging to BPL and BC(A) families: Monthly Stipend for BPL Students
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to students from BPL students to support their education. Under this scheme, a monthly stipend is provided to students studying in classes 1 to 12 in Haryana Government schools.

schemeName: Transgender Utthan Kosh - Self Employment Programme for Transgender
briefDescription: The scheme objective is to provide financial assistance to transgender individuals for self-employment/business establishment. Eligible beneficiaries can receive up to ₹50,000/- or 25% of the project cost.

schemeName: Transgender Utthan Kosh - Training Programme for Transgender
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support the socio-economic development of transgender individuals by providing financial assistance, skill training, and employment opportunities.

schemeName: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Antyodaya Parivar Suraksha Yojana (DAYALU-II)
briefDescription: The aim of this scheme is to provide financial assistance to residents of Haryana in case of accidental death or permanent disability occurred due to stray cattle/animal such as such as cow, bull, oxen, donkey, dog bite, mule, nilgai and buffalo that roam freely.

schemeName: Ambedkar International Scholarship - SC
briefDescription: The "Ambedkar International Scholarship - SC" Scheme by the Rajasthan Social Justice and Empowerment Department provides financial assistance to meritorious SC students for pursuing Ph.D. studies in foreign universities in Humanities and Social Sciences.

schemeName: Kisan Parivahan Yojana
briefDescription: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat. It aims to provide financial assistance to farmers for purchasing medium-sized goods vehicles to facilitate the transportation of agricultural produce.

schemeName: Financial Assistance to SC Students for Pursuing Higher Studies Abroad
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support SC students for higher education abroad in fields like Engineering, Sciences, and Humanities. Benefits include up to ₹20,00,000/- for Ph.D. and ₹10,00,000 for Masters, with eligibility criteria including residency and income below ₹8,00,000/-.

schemeName: Scheme to Provide Financial Assistance to Farmers for Purchasing Sanedo Agricultural Equipment
briefDescription: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat. It aims to provide financial assistance to farmers for purchasing Sanedo (Mini Tractor) equipment, which enhances efficiency and reduces labor dependency in farming operations.

schemeName: Scheme for Assistance on Purchase of Smartphone by the Farmers
briefDescription: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Dept., Gujarat. It aims to provide financial assistance to farmers for purchasing smartphones, enabling them to access agricultural technology, weather updates, pest control techniques, and government assistance.

schemeName: Additional Financial Assistance for the Children of Persons Engaged in the Cleaning Profession
briefDescription: This scheme aims to provide additional financial assistance of ₹500/- per month to children of individuals working in cleaning/sanitation profession. This assistance is given to students already receiving a scholarship under Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme, ensuring their continued education.

schemeName: Transgender Utthan Kosh - College/Higher/Technical Educational Fees Refund for Transgender Students
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote higher and technical education among transgender individuals by providing financial assistance for their educational expenses. Under this scheme, transgender students receive a refund of their school, college, higher, or technical education fees.

schemeName: Marriage Assistance Scheme (MPBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance for the marriage of a registered female worker herself (including one-time remarriage) and for up to two daughters of a registered worker. The scheme applies to the marriage of the female beneficiary herself and the legal, adopted, or stepdaughter of worker.

schemeName: Union Territory of Lakshadweep Pension Scheme for Widows
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide monthly financial assistance of ₹1,500/- to widows in the Union Territory of Lakshadweep. It covers widows who have not remarried and have no source of income.

schemeName: Bhavantar Bharpai Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme is implemented by the Department of Horticulture, Government of Haryana, aims to protect horticultural farmers from market price fluctuations and compensate them if they receive low prices for their produce in the market.

schemeName: Free Supply of Text Books
briefDescription: The scheme provides textbooks or cash subsidies for textbooks and writing materials to students of Government and Aided Schools. Benefits include financial assistance ranging from ₹550/- to ₹830/- per annum, with additional support for geometry boxes. All students from Nursery to 12th are eligible.

schemeName: Diesel VAT Relief Scheme for Fishermen
briefDescription: The "Diesel VAT Relief Scheme for Fishermen" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, aims to provide financial assistance by subsidizing the VAT levied on high-speed diesel used in fishing boats.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Atyant Pichhda Varg Medhavriti Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide ₹10,000/- as a one-time scholarship to extremely backward class students passing class 10th in the first division from the Bihar School Examination Board.

schemeName: Establishment of 1 to 20 Milch Animal Farm
briefDescription: The "Establishment of 1 to 20 Milch Animal Farm" scheme introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, provides financial assistance in the form of an interest subsidy to livestock farmers to take up animal husbandry as a sustainable business.

schemeName: Scholarship for Children of Ex-Servicemen for Vocational and Technical Education
briefDescription: Under this scheme, a scholarship of ₹15,000 per year is provided to the children of ex-servicemen, from the rank of Sepoy to Havildar, who are pursuing vocational & technical education & have not received scholarship assistance under the Prime Minister's Scholarship Scheme or from any other source.

schemeName: Intensive Castration Scheme
briefDescription: The "Intensive Castration Scheme" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, aims to control the stray animal population by castrating male calves and helps in reducing accidents caused by stray male animals in rural and urban areas.

schemeName: Assistance of Concentrate Feed After Parturition (Scheduled Caste Category)
briefDescription: The scheme "Assistance of Concentrate Feed After Parturition (Scheduled Caste Category)" provides 150 kg of BIS Type II concentrate feed to Scheduled Caste Category livestock owners after parturition to support cattle health, increase milk production, and enhance calf development.

schemeName: Assistance of Concentrate Feed for Pregnant Animals (General Category)
briefDescription: This scheme provides free BIS type II concentrate feed to pregnant cattle owned by General Category livestock owners in Gujarat to ensure their good health, boost immunity, prevent metabolic diseases, and enhance milk production.

schemeName: BC & EBC Girls Residential +2 High School
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide quality education and holistic development opportunities for girls from backward and extremely backward classes. Free education, accommodation, study material, medical support, and skill development programs are provided.

schemeName: Educational Study Scheme
briefDescription: The "Educational Study Scheme" provides financial assistance in the form of loans for self-supporting graduate-level courses in professional fields such as Medical, Dental, Engineering, Technology, Pharmacy, Architecture, Nursing, Homeopathy, Veterinary, and similar disciplines.

schemeName: Aapki Beti Yojana
briefDescription: Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹2,100/- per year is provided to girls studying in Class 1 to 8 in government schools, belonging to families living below the poverty line (BPL) and whose both or one parent has passed away. Girls studying in Class 9 to 12 receive ₹2,500/- per year.

schemeName: The Goa State Photo Contest And Exhibition Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage local youths in photography and photojournalism, providing cash prizes and exhibition opportunities. The scheme offers cash prizes of ₹20,000/-, ₹16,000/-, and ₹12,000/- in each category. Entries from applicants born and residing in Goa are preferred.

schemeName: Scheme for Upgradation of Gaushala/ Panjrapole
briefDescription: The "Scheme for Upgradation of Gaushala/ Panjrapole"  aims to promote indigenous cow breeds and make cow rearing economically viable.

schemeName: Merit Scholarship: Engineering University
briefDescription: The scheme provides ₹1000/- per month to eligible students for pursuing B.E. from a recognized engineering college in Chhattisgarh.  The applicants can either be students who have passed their 10th and 12th standard examinations, or Diploma holders newly admitted to the second year of B.E.

schemeName: Merit Scholarship: Polytechnic Institutions
briefDescription: "Merit Scholarship: Polytechnic Institutions" is a scholarship scheme by the Technical Education Department, Government of Chhattisgarh. The scheme provides ₹600/- per month to eligible students for pursuing education from recognized polytechnic institutions in Chhattisgarh.

schemeName: Monthly/Family Pension for Freedom Fighters and their Widows of Uttarakhand State
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance in the form of a monthly pension of ₹25,000/- to the freedom fighters of Uttarakhand and to the widows of deceased freedom fighters, in recognition of their contribution to the nation.

schemeName: Rajya Pratibha Khoj Pariksha Chhatravriti Yojana
briefDescription: Under this scheme, students are provided with scholarships or a one-time incentive for pursuing education up to the postgraduate level, after being selected through the State Talent Search Examination organized by the Board of Secondary Education Rajasthan, Ajmer.

schemeName: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (Punjab)
briefDescription: In this scheme, persons aged between 60 and 79 years receive a pension of ₹200/- per month, while those aged 80 years and above receive ₹500/- per month. The scheme applies only to persons belonging to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.

schemeName: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (Punjab)
briefDescription: The "Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)" provides ₹300/- per month to disabled persons (80% or more disability) aged 18 and above. After age 80, the amount increases to ₹500/-. Dwarfs are also eligible.

schemeName: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (Punjab)
briefDescription: "Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)" provides a pension of ₹300/- per month to widows aged 40 years and above. After attaining the age of 80 years, the pension amount increases to ₹500/- per month.

schemeName: Subsidy to Purchase E-Bike for Journalists
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to journalists in Goa for purchasing e-bikes at a subsidised rate. The benefits include making it more convenient for journalists to gather and disseminate news, with the intended beneficiaries being eligible Goa-based journalists.

schemeName: Patrakar Kritadnyata Nidhi Scheme (Journalist Benevolent Fund)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to journalists in Goa for emergencies like medical treatment, marriage, and education. Benefits include aid up to ₹2,00,000/and scholarships for dependents. Eligible journalists must be registered and working in Goa for at least three years.

schemeName: The Goa State Journalist Awards Scheme
briefDescription: "The Goa State Journalist Awards Scheme" aims to encourage journalists to publish commendable news items, articles, or reports, fostering mass awareness. It provides awards, including a cash prize of ₹10,000/-, to eligible journalists in Goa.

schemeName: Travel Grants To Goan Authors
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to enable the Goan writers to come in close contact with the senior writers of a language-region other than their own within India to acquaint them with the writers and the people of that region, and to widen their awareness and sensibility about the country.

schemeName: Rajasthan Varishth Patrakar Samman Yojana
briefDescription: Under this scheme, a monthly financial assistance of ₹15,000/- is provided to full-time accredited journalists who are 60 years of age or older & have served continuously for at least 20 years in daily, weekly, fortnightly newspapers, independent publications, electronic media, or news agencies.

schemeName: Bhajan Pakhawaj Workshops
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to provide knowledge of appropriate and traditional Bhajan Singing and Pakhawaj playing to Bhajan performers through experts from the respective field. Applications are invited by announcement in local print & electronic media. Certificates are awarded to participants.

schemeName: Laado Protsahan Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage the birth of girl children in poor families and promote their holistic development. Under this scheme, financial support ₹1,50,000/- is provided in seven installments to the parents or guardians of girls born in Government/authorized Medical Institutions.

schemeName: Pension Scheme for State Agitators Other Than Category of Agitators Who Went to Jail for 7 Days or got Injured during the Uttarakhand State Movement
briefDescription: The scheme provides a monthly pension of ₹4,500/- to state agitators not imprisoned for 7 days or injured during the Uttarakhand State Movement, who are not eligible for any other government pension and are not employed in government service.

schemeName: Samajik Sewa Bhatta Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support traditional faith healers by providing a monthly pension. Under this scheme, ₹1,500 is provided to each eligible traditional healer across the state.

schemeName: Tiatr Cantaram Singing Competition
briefDescription: The "Tiatr Cantaram Singing Competition", organised by the Kala Academy under the Department of Art, Culture and Museums, Government of Goa, aims to promote singing talent, preserve and revive the tradition of Tiatr songs, and encourage both traditional and new compositions that reflect Goa.

schemeName: Organisation of Cultural Programs
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to cultural groups and artists registered with the Department for performing in cultural programs organized during traditional fairs, festivals, regional celebrations within and outside the state, as well as on the occasion of national festivals.

schemeName: ICSSR Doctoral Fellowship
briefDescription: The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR) offers Doctoral Fellowships to support full-time Ph.D. scholars engaged in high-quality research in the field of social and human sciences. These fellowships are awarded to Indian research scholars registered in Ph.D. programs at UGC-recognized u

schemeName: Pandit Dindayal Upadhyay Awas Yojana (Developing Caste Welfare) (EBC)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) by offering financial aid up to ₹1,20,000/- for house construction. This scheme also ensures that Economically Weaker Sections receive support in securing shelter.

schemeName: Operation of Sports Hostels
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage tribal students to participate in sports and prepare them for national & international competitions. Tribal students from Classes 6 to 12 are provided with free accommodation, specialized training, nutritious meals & other necessary facilities in the sports hostels.

schemeName: Printing and Free Distribution of Sanskrit Textbooks
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote Sanskrit education by providing free Sanskrit textbooks to students studying in Classes 1 to 12. Under this scheme, annual financial assistance ranging from ₹250/- to ₹700/- is provided to each student enrolled in Classes 1 to 12 of Sanskrit schools.

schemeName: Sero Positive Illness Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme "Sero Positive Illness Scheme" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat aims to provide educational scholarships to orphaned and destitute children affected by Sero Positive illness.

schemeName: Self-Employment Scheme for Scheduled Castes and Tribes
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support scheduled castes and scheduled tribes families below the poverty line through concessional loans and subsidies for self-employment in various sectors.

schemeName: Kailash Darshan Yatra
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance to pilgrims from Gujarat undertaking the Kailash Darshan Yatra. To be eligible, the applicant must be a selected pilgrim from Gujarat and submit proof of yatra completion within 90 days. Applications for this scheme are accepted offline.

schemeName: Sindhu Darshan Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance of ₹15,000/- to pilgrims from Gujarat undertaking the Sindhu Darshan Yatra in Leh-Ladakh, fostering national unity. Intended beneficiaries include native Gujaratis who complete the yatra and apply within 60 days.

schemeName: Varanasi and Amritsar Yatra Scheme
briefDescription: This scheme aims to facilitate pilgrimages to Varanasi and Amritsar for individuals belonging to the Scheduled Castes category. The scheme provides financial reimbursement for second-class train travel, enabling eligible beneficiaries to undertake these religious journeys with ease.

schemeName: State Art Exhibition Competition
briefDescription: The scheme "State Art Exhibition Competition" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat aims to promote fine arts and recognize artistic talents by providing awards and scholarships to artists, art students, and school children across Gujarat.

schemeName: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Medium Scale Ornamental Fish Rearing Unit (Fresh Water) - Haryana
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance in the form of a subsidy is provided to eligible beneficiaries for setting up infrastructure such as sheds, breeding units, and rearing and culture tanks for ornamental fish.

schemeName: PMMSY: Fish Feed Plants of Production Capacity of at least 100 Ton/Day - Haryana
briefDescription: This scheme provides capital subsidy for setting up fish feed plants with a minimum production capacity of 100 tons per day, under the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) to general , schedule caste and women beneficiaries .

schemeName: PMMSY: Establishment of New Freshwater Fin Fish Hatcheries-Haryana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support the establishment of new freshwater finfish hatcheries. Through this scheme, financial assistance in the form of subsidies is provided to beneficiaries for setting up these hatcheries.

schemeName: Mill Gate Price Subsidy Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme provides 15% subsidy on yarn purchases from NHDC depots to handloom weavers, cooperatives, and NGOs, along with freight reimbursement (1–8.5%). Intended beneficiaries include weavers below the poverty line.

schemeName: PMMSY: Live Fish Vending Centres Scheme - Haryana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support fishers by providing financial assistance for live fish vending centers. A subsidy per unit is provided under PMMSY.

schemeName: Handicraft Mela Participation
briefDescription: The scheme enables artisans and handicraft organizations to participate in government-sponsored melas across Gujarat and other states, fostering market access and preserving traditional crafts. Benefits include subsidized stall fees, promotional support, sales opportunities for handmade products.

schemeName: Retired Sportsperson Pension Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme "Retired Sportsperson Pension Scheme" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat aims to ensure financial security to retired state-level medal-winning sportspersons aged above 50 years through monthly pension assistance.

schemeName: Term Loan Scheme (GMFDC)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote self-employment among individuals from the minority communities by providing financial assistance for agriculture and allied activities, small-scale businesses, craftsmanship, transport sector, traditional occupations, and service-based businesses/trades.

schemeName: Educational Loan Scheme (GMFDC)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for technical and non-technical education that creates employment or job opportunities for beneficiaries. Through this scheme, loans are provided to eligible students from minority communities for studies within India and abroad.

schemeName: "Agricultural Income Tax Holiday" under the "Assam Tea Industries Special Incentives Scheme"
briefDescription: The scheme aims to ensure long-term financial sustainability of the Tea industry and to incentivize manufacturers to move from Crush, Tear, Curl tea to Orthodox tea production. The scheme is implemented by the Finance Department and the Industries & Commerce Department, Government of Assam.

schemeName: Handicraft Rebate Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme provides 5–20% rebates on sales of handicraft goods to cooperatives, with higher incentives for women-led groups and during festivals. Applications are submitted online via e-Kutir.

schemeName: Pre-Matric Scholarship for the Students of Socially and Educationally Backward Class
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial aid to promote education. Scholarships are provided to eligible students of Standards 1 to 10.

schemeName: Mahila Samriddhi Yojana (GTKVN)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to empower women entrepreneurs from Thakor & Koli communities by providing access to micro-loans for self-employment. Under this scheme, financial assistance in the form of low-interest loans is provided to eligible beneficiaries to set up small businesses based on their skills.

schemeName: Mahila Samriddhi Yojana (for women only) GNTDNT
briefDescription: The scheme aims to create self-employment for women of nomadic and denotified castes and support women entrepreneurs doing independent business. Through this scheme, microfinance is provided to women beneficiaries to pursue a business/occupation of their choice.

schemeName: Scheme for the Welfare of Schedule Caste Families in Fisheries Sector: Subsidy on Annual Auction of Notified Waters
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to provide full-time employment to individuals from Scheduled Caste families in the fisheries sector and uplift their socio-economic status by promoting fish farming through subsidization of fishing contract costs in designated water bodies.

schemeName: Pashupalan (GBCDC)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide an amount for animal husbandry activities. Low-interest loans are provided to Pashu Palaks.

schemeName: Educational Loan Scheme (GBCDC)
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial support to students from socially and educationally backward classes for higher technical and professional education. It offers loans at concessional interest rates for approved graduate and postgraduate courses like engineering, medical, MBA, MCA, and more.

schemeName: Foreign Study Loan
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to students from non-reserved categories for pursuing higher studies abroad in medical and post-graduation courses. It offers loans up to ₹15,00,000/- at 4% simple interest per annum to eligible students.

schemeName: Testing Equipment Scheme
briefDescription: The Government of Haryana launched the Testing Equipment Assistance Scheme to support Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) in enhancing product quality through financial aid for procuring testing equipment.

schemeName: Protection Against Domestic Violence
briefDescription: The scheme "Protection Against Domestic Violence" by the Women and Child Development Department, Government of Haryana, aims to provide legal, emotional, and social support to women facing domestic violence through grievance redressal, offering protection, counseling, and mediation to victims.

schemeName: Scholarship to SC students studying in selected schools under Talentpool Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide the best education to meritorious Scheduled Caste students in high-quality residential schools to help them build their careers and improve their living standards.

schemeName: Pre-Matric Scholarship for the Students of Notified Tribes and Denotified Tribes Castes
briefDescription: The scheme supports school education by providing annual scholarships to students from Notified Tribes and Denotified Tribes studying in standards 1st to 10th.

schemeName: "Marketing Incentive" under the "Handloom Marketing Assistance" Component of the National Handloom Development Programme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to develop and promote marketing channels for handloom products to bring greater visibility to the sector and ensure commensurate remuneration to weavers. It provides Marketing Incentive to eligible handloom agencies for creating conducive conditions for marketing handloom products.

schemeName: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Construction of New Grow-out Pond - Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to enhance fish production and provide employment through inland fisheries. It offers financial support to construct new grow-out ponds for eligible beneficiaries.

schemeName: Scheme for Integrated Development for Horticulture (HRT-2)
briefDescription: The scheme "Scheme for Integrated Development for Horticulture (HRT-2)" aims to promote holistic horticultural growth through financial assistance for production, processing, marketing, and infrastructure support to farmers across Gujarat.

schemeName: Vishesh Katan, Vankari & Vanat Sahay (Spinning & Weaving Assistance Scheme)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance in addition to the central government assistance to spinners and weavers. It provides production incentives to Khadi spinners and weavers with category-wise differential assistance rates.

schemeName: Sharodotsav Pandit Nandan Mehta: Plan for Conducting Classical Music Competition and Ceremony
briefDescription: The scheme "Sharodotsav Pandit Nandan Mehta: Plan for Conducting Classical Music Competition and Ceremony" aims to promote classical music and percussion by organizing a state-level competition and award ceremony on the death anniversary of Pt. Nandan Mehta, through the provision of cash prizes.

schemeName: National Mission on Edible Oil- Oil Palm: Nursery
briefDescription: The scheme component "Nursery" launched under the "National Mission on Edible Oil–Oil Palm" scheme aims to increase the production of oil palm in the state and reduce dependence on imports through providing subsidy assistance for setting up nurseries for farmers.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemeName: Visvesvaraya PhD Scheme for Electronics and IT: Full Time PhD Candidates
briefDescription: The scheme aims at generating a total of 1000 Full-time PhD Candidates in emerging research areas of Electronic System Design and Manufacturing (ESDM) and IT/IT Enabled Services (ITES) sectors over a period of 5 years.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemeName: Visvesvaraya PhD Scheme for Electronics and IT: Part Time PhD Candidates
briefDescription: The scheme aims at generating a total of 150 Part-time PhDs in emerging research areas of Electronics System Design & Manufacturing (ESDM) and IT/IT Enabled Services (IT/ITES) sectors over a period of 5 years.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemeName: Visvesvaraya PhD Scheme for Electronics and IT: Young Faculty Research Fellowships
briefDescription: This fellowship is meant for Young Faculty having regular position at their institution and proven track record in research. Support is provisioned for 50 candidates, for an initial period of support of 2 years, extendible till 5 years based on performance review.

schemeName: Financial Assistance as Interest Subsidy on Home Loan
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide 50% reimbursement of interest on home loans up to ₹1,00,000 for eligible war widows, war bereaved families, war disabled personnel, and attributable peacetime casualties.

schemeName: Kishori Balika Yojana- Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to create awareness among adolescent girls aged 14 to 18 years about nutrition, health, and life cycle issues, and also provides supplementary nutrition for 300 days in a year.

schemeName: National Mission on Edible Oil–Oil Palm: Planting Materials
briefDescription: The scheme component "Planting Materials" launched under the "National Mission on Edible Oil–Oil Palm" scheme aims to increase oil palm production in the state and reduce dependency on imports through financial assistance to farmers for procuring planting materials.

schemeName: Shridev Suman State Merit Scholarship
briefDescription: The scheme provides monthly scholarship of ₹1,000/- to meritorious students who qualify in eligibility test conducted by SCERT Uttarakhand. The scholarship is awarded to the next 5 students from each development block of Uttarakhand who are not selected for the Dr. Shivanand Nautiyal Scholarship.

schemeName: Assistance in Trellise Mandap for Cultivation of Vegetable Crops
briefDescription: The scheme "Assistance in Trellise Mandap for Cultivation of Vegetable Crops" aims to promote structured vegetable farming through the provision of financial assistance for constructing kachha/semi-pucca/pucca trellis mandaps to all farmers across the state.

schemeName: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Water Carrying Pipe Line (TASP & OTASP) Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to improve the agricultural productivity of Scheduled Tribe farmers through subsidized seeds, fertilizers, and equipment. It provides direct support to tribal farmers for enhancing crop production and livelihood.

schemeName: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Tarpaulin Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to enhance crop production and livelihood. It provides subsidies on seeds, fertilizers, tarpaulins, and other agricultural inputs to Scheduled Tribe farmers.

schemeName: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Castor cake/Neem cake/ Liquid Fermented Organic Manure / Green manure seed (For mix in the soil) Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to help tribal farmers improve their livelihood by increasing agricultural crop production. Through this scheme, subsidies are provided on high-yield and hybrid seeds, fertilizers, and other agricultural inputs to Scheduled Tribe farmers.

schemeName: Integrated Horticulture Development Program for Tribal Area Farmers (TASP) HRT-3
briefDescription: The scheme "Integrated Horticulture Development Program for Tribal Area Farmers (TASP) HRT-3" by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to enhance the horticulture sector through financial assistance and subsidies to tribal area farmers across the state.

schemeName: PMMSY: Three wheeler with Ice Box including e-rickshaws for fish vending - Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to strengthen fish storage infrastructure for hygienic preservation. Through this scheme, beneficiaries receive financial assistance for a three-wheeler with an Ice Box, including e-rickshaws for fish vending.

schemeName: PMMSY: Support for Acquisition of Deep Sea Fishing Vessels for Traditional Fishermen - Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to strengthen fish storage infrastructure for hygienic preservation. Through this scheme, beneficiaries receive financial assistance for the support for acquisition of deep-sea fishing vessels for traditional fishermen.
tags: Support For Acquisition Of Deep Sea Fishing Vessels

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Medhavi Chhatra Puraskar Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage academic excellence among students and provides cash prizes to those who secure first, second, or third positions in their graduation or post-graduation studies at the university level.

schemeName: Rishi and Milan Khosla Scholarship Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote higher education among girl students in the state by providing scholarships to those pursuing their first year of post-graduation in various government colleges across Uttarakhand.

schemeName: Khiladi Protsahan Chhatravriti Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote sports among talented youth sportspersons aged 14 to 23 years from Uttarakhand by providing a scholarship of ₹2,000/- per month and financial assistance of ₹10,000/- once a year for sports equipment.

schemeName: Funeral Assistance Scheme for Unorganized Workers (Except Construction Workers)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to heirs of deceased unorganized sector workers for funeral expenses. It offers ₹10,000/- to eligible heirs of e-Shram card holders.

schemeName: Sanskrit Chhatra Pratibha Samman
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage and motivate students to pursue higher studies in Sanskrit by rewarding those who achieve top positions in the subject at the high school, intermediate, undergraduate, and postgraduate levels.

schemeName: PMMSY: Construction of  Plant/Storage of Minimum 20 Tonne Capacity - Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial support for the construction of 20-tonne capacity ice plant/storage units, ensuring hygienic fish storage and improving the income of beneficiaries.

schemeName: PMMSY: Construction of  Plant/Storage of Minimum 30 Tonne Capacity - Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to strengthen fish storage infrastructure for hygienic preservation. Through this scheme, beneficiaries receive financial assistance for constructing 30-tonne capacity plants or storage units.

schemeName: Saksham Scholarship
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support meritorious differently-abled students pursuing graduate-level studies in the state by providing them with financial assistance in the form of scholarships.

schemeName: PMMSY: Motorcycle with Ice Box - Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for the purchase of motorcycles with ice boxes. Through this scheme, eligible beneficiaries receive subsidies to enhance their livelihood opportunities.

schemeName: Vidhya Sadhana Yojana Gujarat
briefDescription: Under this scheme, free bicycles are gifted to girl students across Gujarat to reduce school dropouts among scheduled tribe girls after class 8.

schemeName: Sri Ram Janmabhoomi Shabari Smriti Yatra Sahay Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to scheduled tribes and tribal communities for pilgrimage to Sri Ram Janmabhoomi in Ayodhya in memory of Maa Shabari. The scheme offers ₹5,000/- financial assistance per pilgrim to enable darshan of Lord Sri Ram for eligible tribal beneficiaries.

schemeName: AYUSH Health and Wellness Centre - Ayushman Bharat Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide accessible, affordable, and holistic healthcare services based on Ayurveda, Yoga, Unani and Homeopathy systems to people of all age groups.

schemeName: Sponsorship Scheme (90% Centrally Funded)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to children upto age of 18 years, including those who have lost both parents, those whose main earning parent has died, & children who have suffered due to violation of laws, child labor, child trafficking, child marriage & similar circumstances.

schemeName: Higher Education Assistance Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage children of workers and reduce financial burden of education through higher educational assistance. It provides ₹20,000/- for Bachelor of Medicine Bachelor of Surgery admission and ₹10,000/- for other graduate courses to merit students.

schemeName: Promotion of Tiatr Music
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage individuals to learn blowing instruments crucial for Tiatr music, such as Trumpet & Saxophone, addressing a decline in musicians. It provides financial assistance for tuition and instrument purchase, benefiting youth and other interested persons to preserve Tiatr music.

schemeName: Documentation And Research Assistantship Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage documentation and digitisation of valuable Tiatr-related materials to prevent their loss to posterity. It provides assistance and incentives to holders of such materials and pays honorarium to documentation assistants.

schemeName: Low Altitude Guide Course
briefDescription: The scheme aims to train and certify youth from Uttarakhand as professional low altitude guides and provide them with skill development opportunities to strengthen their role in the state’s tourism sector.

schemeName: 6% Interest Subsidy on Loans taken through Banks for Study Abroad
briefDescription: The scheme aims to reduce financial stress for ST students pursuing foreign education by providing up to ₹1,50,000 subsidy on interest for 3 years.

schemeName: National Livestock Mission: Livestock Insurance Scheme - Uttarakhand Livestock Development Board
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide insurance coverage to livestock in order to protect cattle rearers against the loss of their animals due to death, thereby encouraging them to adopt better animal husbandry practices.

schemeName: Maitri Prashikshan Karyakram - Uttarakhand Livestock Development Board
briefDescription: The scheme aims to enhance livestock productivity and improve  artificial insemination services across the state through the training and capacity building of local youth.

schemeName: Machhali Palan
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial support to fishermen for repairing, reconstructing, and replacing damaged boats and nets due to disasters.

schemeName: Financial Assistance to Dramatic Troupes participating in Kala Academy’s Annual Tiatr Competition
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage non-commercial dramatic troupes participating in Kala Academy’s Annual Tiatr Competition to produce higher quality Tiatrs. It provides financial support for various production aspects, video recording, and expert workshops, benefiting dramatic troupes.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for encouraging production of Traditional Khell-Tiatr
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage and preserve the traditional Khell-Tiatr folk musical drama of Goa by promoting quality productions. It offers financial assistance and infrastructure support for staging shows, including video documentation, benefiting dramatic troupes and individuals.

schemeName: Enhancement of Spoken English Programme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to enhance the spoken English skills of students of Classes 9 to 12 studying in all government schools across the districts of the state of Uttarakhand.

schemeName: Amit Gautam Memorial Scholarship (B.Tech in Electronics Engineering – 4th Year)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support meritorious students pursuing a B.Tech in Electronics Engineering who are in their 4th year of study by providing a one-year scholarship amounting to ₹7,000/-.

schemeName: Hands-on Training and Exposure Visits under Experiential Learning
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide free one-week residential hands-on certificate training programs to undergraduate, postgraduate students, researchers, and faculty members, including accommodation and meals.

schemeName: One-day Seminar on Vedic Mathematics
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide a common platform for officers, teachers, and students from universities, colleges, schools, and other departments to actively participate and gain knowledge about the simplified curriculum of Vedic Mathematics and its easy-to-apply methods.

schemeName: Exhibition of Books on Tiatr, Its Song and Music
briefDescription: The scheme aims to help Roman Script Konkani writers and publishers who face difficulties in marketing their books related to Tiatr, its song and music, while promoting Tiatr culture among enthusiasts. The scheme provides bulk purchase of books at 80% of the fixed rate and organizes book exhibition.

schemeName: Ashramshalas (Grant in Aid)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide schooling, boarding and lodging to Scheduled Tribe students at a single location, enabling better employment opportunities and competitive equality with other sections of society. The scheme offers residential education with complete support including free education.

schemeName: Establishment and Operation of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) Laboratories
briefDescription: The scheme aims to establish STEM laboratories in schools across Uttarakhand to provide students of classes 6 to 12 with hands-on science, technology, engineering, and mathematics learning opportunities and strengthen their practical understanding of scientific concepts.

schemeName: Empowering Schedule Tribe/Schedule Caste Communities Using Information And Communication Technology Tools
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide Information Technology related trainings free of cost to the Schedule Tribe (ST) and Schedule Caste (SC) communities. Benefits include basic computer skills and job-oriented Information Technology training courses, intended for citizens, students, local youth, etc.

schemeName: Scheme To Provide Grant To The Institution(s)/Individual(s) For Long Term Project In The Field Of Art And Culture
