briefDescription: The scheme aims to give financial support to institution(s)/individual(s) involved in long term projects in the field of Art and Culture. It provides a maximum grant of ₹5,00,000/- per annum to eligible organizations and individuals, while also encouraging young talent and offering scholarships.

schemeName: Scheme To Provide Assistance To Students Seeking Education Outside Goa In Any Field Of Art And Culture
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage young talents in Goa to achieve the highest possible standard in fields of Art and Culture by providing financial support for training outside the State. Assistance is provided to eligible students for basic or advanced education in art.

schemeName: Promotion of Acting in Tiatr for School Children
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage school children to improve their acting abilities in Goan tiatr through organized competitions. It provides cash prizes and certificates to winners in comedy and non-comedy acting categories.

schemeName: Goa State Logistics and Warehousing Incentives Scheme: Subsidy Towards Annual Interest Rate Payable On Loans
briefDescription: The subsidy aims to support the logistics and warehousing sector by reimbursing 50% of the annual interest paid on construction loans. The benefit is capped at ₹25,00,000/- (Indian Rupees Twenty-five Lakhs) annually per project and is available for up to 05 consecutive years for eligible entities.

schemeName: Compensation for Crop Damage Caused by Wildlife (in Revenue and Forest Villages)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide timely financial assistance to farmers for crop damage caused by wildlife, assessed and compensated within 30 working days as per revenue book circular guidelines.

schemeName: Development of Manures & Fertilizers: Assistance for Construction/Installation of Vermi-Compost Units
briefDescription: The "Vermi-compost Units" component of the scheme aims to convert available on-field and farm waste material into chemical-free farm resources as farm input for organic farming through vermicomposting processes. The payment of the sanctioned subsidy is through Electronic Clearance System (ECS).

schemeName: Bhagwan Birsa Munda Lakshya Siddhi Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance, in the form of a Scholarship, proportionate to the fees paid by Scheduled Tribe students at the Higher Secondary School Certificate (HSSC) level in order to take offline coaching assistance through reputed institutions for preparation for NEET & JEE.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Bal Hridaya Upchar Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance ranging from ₹25,000/- to ₹1,00,000/- for treatment, covering 07 identified heart diseases in children. Key features include covering costs for treatment at recognized government or private hospitals.

schemeName: Training/Capacity Building Programmes/Research work on Remote Sensing and GIS Applications
briefDescription: The scheme aims to strengthen scientific and technical capacity of researchers, students, and government personnel in the field of Remote Sensing and Geographic Information System (GIS) applications by providing training, conducting capacity-building programmes, and supporting research initiatives.

schemeName: Kalakar Kritadnyata Nidhi: Pandemics
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to aged and needy Goan artists who have lost the source of income due to the pandemic. The scheme provides relief in the form of financial assistance amounting to a min. of ₹5,000/- (lumpsum) up to a max. of ₹10,000/- (lumpsum) to eligible artists.

schemeName: Free Uniforms for Students
briefDescription: This scheme Launched on 1 July 2004 with the aims to ensure regular attendance and continuity in education by offering uniform assistance to both boys and girls enrolled in Classes 1 to 8.

schemeName: Hybrid Chilli Production Program
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide self-employment opportunities and increase income for Scheduled Caste farmers living below the poverty line by promoting hybrid chilli cultivation.

schemeName: Saral Bijli Bill Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme offers free domestic connections and a fixed monthly charge to ensure easy electricity access for eligible beneficiaries.

schemeName: Scheme for Persons with Benchmark Disability Requiring High Support
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote economic self-reliance and ensure the well-being of persons with benchmark disabilities requiring high support. It provides a one-time financial assistance of ₹40,000/- to eligible applicants who are bonafide residents of Goa and belong to the EWS Category.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Sudharit Kamdhenu Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to help the farmers to get financial assistance for the purchase of cross bred cows, improved she buffaloes and cows of indigenous breeds namely Sahiwal, Gir, Red Sindhi, Rathi and Tharparkar for their Dairy Unit, to promote self employment in the Dairy Sector.

schemeName: Sanitary Napkin Distribution - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote menstrual hygiene and prevent reproductive tract infections among adolescent girls aged 10 to 19 years in Uttarakhand by providing sanitary napkins at a nominal cost of ₹6/-, available through all ASHA workers across the state.

schemeName: Registration for Crop Procurement
briefDescription: The scheme aims to ensure fair crop prices for farmers by facilitating government procurement and provides easy online registration for landholding farmers to sell their crops at minimum support price.

schemeName: Raah-Veer Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme encourages citizens to help road accident victims during emergencies by providing a ₹25,000 incentive to individuals who save lives through timely medical assistance.

schemeName: Eja-Boi Shagun Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote the safe health and well-being of mothers and newborns by encouraging institutional deliveries and ensuring that mothers stay in government hospitals for at least 48 hours after childbirth.

schemeName: Immunization Programme - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: This initiative aims to protect every child from preventable, life-threatening diseases by providing free and timely vaccination as per the National Immunization Schedule.

schemeName: ST Girl Child Literacy Incentive Amount - Class 11th
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Tribe girl students of Madhya Pradesh enrolled in Class 11 to encourage them to continue their education and prevent dropouts.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Ghasyari Kalyan Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide affordable and nutritious packed silage and total mixed animal feed to livestock farmers, especially women, in the remote hilly areas of Uttarakhand. Under this scheme, silage is made available to livestock farmers at a subsidized rate of ₹2.75 per kg.

schemeName: Award of Cash Incentive to the Toppers at Regional Level and Students Scoring Centum in the Subjects Involving Theory in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Public Examinations in Government Schools in the Union Territory of Puducherry
briefDescription: The scheme aims to award cash incentives to encourage students in their education by recognizing top three regional rank holders and those scoring centum in theory subjects in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Exams in Govt. Schools.

schemeName: Tribals Development Plan: Subsidy for Purchase of Motorcycle
briefDescription: The scheme aims to assist tribal families in Goa to purchase motorcycles for self-employment by offering a government subsidy, thereby promoting economic self-reliance among low-income households.

schemeName: Tribals Development Plan: Motor Driving Training [Light & Heavy Vehicles]
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide full financial coverage for motor driving training, including courses for Light Motor Vehicles and Heavy Motor Vehicles, specifically recognizing Goa’s future in the transport sector. It benefits eligible Tribal families in Goa whose annual income is below ₹1,20,000/-.

schemeName: Various Training and Support Schemes of Kisan Vigyan Kendra - Veer Chandra Singh Garhwali Uttarakhand University of Horticulture and Forestry
briefDescription: The scheme aims to enhance the knowledge, skills, and productivity of farmers in Uttarakhand by providing them with the latest technical training in agriculture, horticulture, and forestry.

schemeName: Tea Development Programme in the Hilly Districts of Uttarakhand - Uttarakhand Tea Development Board
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote tea cultivation in the mountainous regions of Uttarakhand, providing sustainable employment, optimal utilization of barren and unused land, and economic upliftment of local farmers.

schemeName: Grant For Oru Kala Pooja
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide annual financial assistance to Hindu Religious Institutions situated in the Union Territory of Puducherry that have poor resources. It benefits the temple authorities by covering expenses associated with the performance of Oru Kala Pooja, Current Consumption Charges, etc.

schemeName: District Plan: Inter-Village Road Construction Scheme on Contributory Basis
briefDescription: The scheme aims to facilitate the easy and timely transportation of sugarcane from farms to purchasing centers and sugar mills by improving inter-village road connectivity in sugarcane-growing areas.

schemeName: Grid Connected Rooftop Solar Power Plant Scheme - Uttarakhand Renewable Energy Development Agency
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote the adoption of rooftop solar energy among domestic consumers in Uttarakhand by providing financial support and enabling them to generate clean, renewable electricity, reduce their electricity bills through net metering.

schemeName: Tourist Guide Certification Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to train and certify Goan youth as tourist guides through Basic and Advanced modules. It reimburses fees for the Basic course and sponsors the Advanced curriculum, creating employment and entrepreneurial opportunities in Goa’s tourism sector.

schemeName: Grant Scheme for Cultivation of Aromatic Plants - Centre for Aromatic Plants
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage farmers to adopt the cultivation of commercially valuable aromatic crops such as lemongrass, citronella, palmarosa, khus, damask rose, mint (except Japanese mint), geranium, black cumin, rosemary, bay leaf, timur & sandalwood by providing financial assistance to them.

schemeName: Financial Assistance For Renovation And Special Repair Works To Hindu Religious Institutions Situated In Economically Weaker Section Areas / Adi-Dravidar Colonies
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide one-time financial assistance of ₹2,50,000/- for renovation and special repairs, specifically targeting the purchase of building materials. The intended beneficiaries are Hindu religious institutions situated in Economically Weaker Section Areas or Adi-Dravidar Colonies.

schemeName: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar’s Yatra Scheme to visit the places connected with Dr. B.R. Ambedkar’s life in India
briefDescription: The scheme aims to assist the people who want to visit the places connected with Dr. B.R. Ambedkar’s life in India. It provides comprehensive financial assistance, with all expenses for the tour programme fully borne by the Department, targeting SC/ST applicants of Puducherry.

schemeName: Uttarakhand Mega Industrial and Investment Policy - 2021
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote large-scale industrial development and investment in the state of Uttarakhand by creating a favourable business environment, encouraging capital investment, and generating employment opportunities.

schemeName: Tea Tourism - Uttarakhand Tea Development Board
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote tea tourism in Uttarakhand by attracting visitors to tea gardens and factories, creating local jobs, and supporting the state’s tea industry.

schemeName: Employment Subsidy Scheme, 2017
briefDescription: The scheme aims to create an environment conducive for attracting investment in Goa, incentivise existing units, and boost local employment. It provides a subsidy based on the percentage of Goan employees hired, restricting reimbursement to ₹7,000/- per Goan employee per month.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for ANM/GNM/Technical Courses
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide assistance to the youths of the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community for training in job-assured technical and professional Diploma and Degree courses. It supports training for courses like ANM, GNM, and Para-Medical, with a minimum duration of one year.

schemeName: Grants To Patients Suffering From Tuberculosis/Cancer And Other Major Diseases
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for medical treatment to patients suffering from Tuberculosis, Cancer, and other major diseases. It provides a one-time financial grant to patients of the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community.

schemeName: The Goa State Incentives to Encourage Investments Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to create sustainable employment, accelerate investment, and support environmentally sustainable industrial development in Goa. It provides financial assistance and incentives under separate target schemes to new and existing Micro, Small, Medium, and Large manufacturing units.

schemeName: Craft & Gear (Unit: Boats of Appropriate Sizes Including Nets, Fish & Ice Holding Boxes, Etc.)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to uplift traditional fishermen by providing financial help in the form of a subsidy on the purchase or construction of new wooden or Fiberglass Reinforced Plastic fishing crafts below 26 feet for inland fishing activities in Goa.

schemeName: Simon Sing Horo Special Post-Matric Scholarship
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide a one-time incentive of ₹10,000/- to students of the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community who have cleared High School Leaving Certificate or Higher Secondary School Leaving Certificate examinations during the current year.

schemeName: Providing Model Maps for Residential Construction
briefDescription: It aims to provide landowners in Haridwar district with model maps for residential construction according to the size of their plots.

schemeName: Map Simplification Process
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide an efficient online system for landowners in Haridwar district to get approval for their residential and commercial property maps quickly and easily, ensuring transparency and reducing delays.

schemeName: Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Urban Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NULM): Self-Help Group - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to empower urban poor women in Uttarakhand by promoting the formation of self-help groups, improving their livelihoods, and supporting them in income-generating and self-employment activities through financial incentives, bank loans, and government subsidies.

schemeName: Incentives To Encourage Purchases From Local Suppliers Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage consumption through local purchases and support the economies of ancillary units, local Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, and traders. It provides a reimbursement of 2% (up to ₹5,00,000/- annually) on eligible local purchases to eligible manufacturing units in Goa.

schemeName: Veerjyoti Puraskars
briefDescription: The scheme aims to recognize and award women and girls in the State of Goa who have shown exceptional bravery and courage against atrocities, violence, and/or harassment. It provides a cash prize of ₹20,000/- and a scroll/citation to two awardees annually.

schemeName: Greenhouse Construction
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote protected cultivation of vegetables and flowers by encouraging farmers to adopt greenhouse technology through substantial financial assistance and support, thereby increasing productivity, improving crop quality, and enhancing farmers’ income.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Venture Capital Fund for Scheduled Castes
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote entrepreneurship among Scheduled Caste individuals by providing concessional finance. Benefits include investment ranging from ₹10,00,000/- to ₹15,00,00,000/- as debt or equity for eligible Private Limited or Public Limited companies.

schemeName: Short Term Financial Assistance To Good Borrowers
briefDescription: The scheme aims to meet short term financial requirements related to executing specific orders, seasonal fund increases, or firm export orders. Benefits include loans ranging from ₹5,00,000/- to ₹50,00,000/-, primarily targeting existing Medium Scale Industry and Small Scale Industry units.

schemeName: Scheme for Cottage and Village Industries (Composite Loan Scheme)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to extend composite financial assistance (term loan and/or working capital loan) to Artisans, Village and Cottage Industries, and Micro Enterprises promoted by residents of Puducherry. Benefits include loans up to ₹25,00,000/- per unit.

schemeName: Equipment Refinance Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to financially assist the well performing SSI units for the purchase of identifiable items of equipment / machinery for diversification / modernization / expansion / replacement and also balancing equipment. The applications for this scheme are accepted online through PIPDIC portal.

schemeName: Nishadraj Scholarship Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote higher education among children of fisher families working in federation reservoirs by providing scholarships of up to ₹20,000 for technical courses and up to ₹10,000 for non-technical courses.

schemeName: 108, Emergency Ambulance Service - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide timely emergency medical assistance by transporting patients to hospitals and offering essential first aid through a free, state-wide ambulance service accessible by calling 108.

schemeName: Old Age Pension Scheme - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹1,500/- is provided to senior citizens (both husband and wife) in the state who are 60 years of age or above and whose family’s monthly income is up to ₹4,000/- or who belong to the BPL category.

schemeName: Gunavatta Evam Navaachar Nishulk Paathy Pustak Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide free textbooks to all students from Classes 1 to 8 studying in government and government-aided schools, as well as recognized madrasas that follow the state government curriculum.

schemeName: Divyang Pension Scheme - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support persons with disabilities in meeting their basic needs and improving their quality of life. Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹1,500/- is provided to persons with disabilities who are above18 years of age & have a disability of more than 40%.

schemeName: Sanitary Napkin Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide sanitary napkins to adolescent girls and women at subsidized rates through Anganwadi centres across the state. Under this scheme, sanitary napkins are made available to adolescent girls and women at a cost of ₹6.00 through Anganwadi centres.

schemeName: Dwarf Pension Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial support to individuals suffering from dwarfism to help them meet their basic needs and improve their quality of life. Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹1200/- is provided to persons above 21 years of age whose height is less than 4 feet.

schemeName: Khushiyon Ki Sawari Sewa - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide safe, timely, and free transportation for pregnant women and newborn babies (0 - 1 year) to access healthcare services, including travel from home to the hospital and from the hospital back home.

schemeName: Maulana Azad Education Finance Foundation Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide interest-free loans of up to ₹5 lakh to students from minority communities for pursuing higher education in India and abroad.

schemeName: Scholarship for Engineering and Medical Education
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support the higher education of dependents of ex-servicemen by providing financial assistance for pursuing engineering, medical, and PhD courses. Under this scheme, annual scholarships are awarded to beneficiaries based on their course of study.

schemeName: Uchcha Shiksha Protsahan Chhatravriti Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote higher education among meritorious students by providing financial support to regular undergraduate and postgraduate students in government colleges and state universities of Uttarakhand.

schemeName: Admission Process in All Government and Non-Government Schools of the State
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide free and accessible education for children from Class 1 to 8 by offering free education and essential learning materials, including books, shoe-bags, and meals, across all government and non-government schools in the state.

schemeName: Scholarship on Merit Basis
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage meritorious students pursuing undergraduate and postgraduate studies at Uttarakhand Sanskrit University by awarding scholarships to those who secure the highest marks in the relevant course, class, or subject.

schemeName: Self-Employment Scheme for Unemployed Minority Communities
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide a grant of 25% of the loan amount (for loans ranging from ₹1 lakh to ₹10 lakh) to individuals belonging to minority communities, who are permanent residents of Uttarakhand, for the purpose of self-employment.

schemeName: Adventure Training for Youth
briefDescription: The scheme aims to empower the youth of Uttarakhand by providing free adventure training programs that develop their skills and capabilities through various adventure activities.

schemeName: Girl Education Promotion (Bicycle Scheme)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote girls' education in the state by providing financial assistance for the purchase of bicycles to girl students studying in Class 9 in government and government-aided secondary schools.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Udeeyman Khiladi Unnayan Chhatravriti Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote sports among talented children aged 8 to 14 years in Uttarakhand by providing a monthly scholarship of ₹1,500. Players in this age group are selected every year on the basis of Physical & Sports Aptitude Test (P-SAT), with 150 boys & 150 girls selected from each district.

schemeName: Special Incentive Scheme for Women of Tharu, Boksa and Other Tribes (For Training and Marketing)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to empower tribal women artisans through specialized training and marketing opportunities and ensures the provision of financial assistance for travel, logistics, and stall rent for participating artisans.

schemeName: Dr. A.N. Mukhopadhyay Needy Student Fund (B.Sc. Agriculture - Final Year Student)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance of ₹25,000/- per year to meritorious & needy students pursuing B.Sc. Agriculture course in their final year of study, based on their genuine financial need, thereby helping to reduce their economic burden & support the continuation of their education.

schemeName: Old Age Pension Scheme - On Completion of 60 Years (UKBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide a monthly pension of ₹1,000/- to construction workers registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board who have attained the age of 60 years.

schemeName: Assistance to Handloom Artisans - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial support to disaster-affected handloom artisans for the repair or repurchase of damaged tools, equipment, raw materials, and finished products.

schemeName: Cash Prize to Players and their Coaches for Winning Medals in National and International Competitions
briefDescription: The scheme aims to recognize and honor players and their coaches from the state who have achieved remarkable success and won medals at the national and international level in the field of sports. There is a provision to award prize money ranging from a minimum of ₹10,000/- to a maximum of ₹2 crore.

schemeName: Grant for Sex-Sorted Semen to Livestock Farmers
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote breed improvement and increase milk production by encouraging the use of sex-sorted semen for artificial insemination in cows and buffaloes.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Swarozgar Yojana - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote self-employment and entrepreneurship among the residents of Uttarakhand by providing financial assistance and subsidies for establishing new enterprises or upgrading existing small-scale units.

schemeName: Nishulk Swasthya Jaanch Yojana - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to ensure accessible, affordable and quality healthcare services for all citizens of Uttarakhand by providing free diagnostic tests across government healthcare facilities.

schemeName: Incentive Scheme for Marrying a Disabled Young Man/Woman
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance of ₹25,000/- to couples on marriage, where at least one partner has 40% or more disability and marries a non-disabled person, or where both partners have disabilities.

schemeName: Special Financial Assistance Scheme for Meritorious Students of the State for Preparation of Competitive Examinations
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial support of ₹50,000/- to meritorious students who have cleared the preliminary examination conducted by the State or Union Public Service Commission for Group 'A' and 'B' services, or the written examination for the Armed Forces (e.g., NDA, CDS, OTA, etc.).

schemeName: Training Programme for Entrepreneurship Development in Biotechnology
briefDescription: The scheme aims to train youth in biotechnology and develop entrepreneurial skills by providing technical knowledge, mentorship, and guidance to help them initiate biotech startups.

schemeName: State Fisheries Input Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to aims to enhance the productivity and income of fish farmers by providing financial support in the form of subsidies on essential fishery inputs such as hand nets, hapa, nets, mini kits, etc.

schemeName: Science Communication and Popularization
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote scientific thinking and awareness among citizens by providing support and funding to educational institutions, R&D organizations, and NGOs to organize science communication activities.

schemeName: Aromatic Awareness Programme - Centre for Aromatic Plants
briefDescription: The scheme aims to raise awareness about aromatic plants and their benefits, and to provide guidance on cultivation practices suitable to different agricultural climates.

schemeName: Very Dense Apple Plantation Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote intensive apple cultivation among farmers by establishing modern orchards with high-yielding varieties and providing financial assistance through a 60% subsidy on saplings, thereby enhancing productivity and increasing farmers’ income.

schemeName: Establishment of Vermicompost Units
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote sustainable agriculture and organic farming by providing financial assistance for setting up vermicompost units to farmers and women self-help groups.

schemeName: Udhyan Card
briefDescription: It aims to create a unified identification system for farmers engaged in horticulture activities, enabling them to access all horticulture-related schemes and benefits provided by the Government of Uttarakhand.

schemeName: Refinance Scheme for Technology Development and Modernization
briefDescription: The scheme aims to assist Sole Proprietorships, Partnerships, Co-operative Societies, Private and Public Limited Companies for meeting the expenditure on purchase of capital equipment, acquisition of technical know-how, and the upgradation of process technology and products.

schemeName: Priyank Pathak Scholarship for B.Sc. Agriculture (4th Year) Students
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to meritorious students pursuing a B.Sc. in Agriculture who are in their fourth year of study, by awarding them a monthly scholarship of ₹ 800/- for one year.

schemeName: Scheme for Acquisition of Equipment and Others
briefDescription: The scheme aims to assist organizations in the small-scale sector in acquiring essential infrastructure and technology upgrades. The scheme provides need-based financial assistance for in-house quality control facilities, DG sets, pollution control equipment, computers and related accessories.

schemeName: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Sahkarita Kisan Kalyan Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide interest-free loans to farmers and self-help groups for agricultural and non-agricultural activities to promote self-employment and strengthen the rural economy.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Sampoorna Pushti Yojana: Maternal Nutrition
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide supplementary Take Home Ration (THR) in the form of nutrient-rich food products to pregnant women and lactating mothers across the state.

schemeName: Aama Kuni Pilaa
briefDescription: The scheme aims to focus on early stimulation and responsive parenting in the best interest of each child from 0-3 years. The scheme envisages promoting parent-led, home-based, joyful child-friendly learning by creating an early stimulating environment at the household level.

schemeName: Kalpana Chawla Chatravriti Yojana
briefDescription: Launched in 2012-13 by the Govt. of Himachal Pradesh, the "Kalpana Chawla Chatravriti Yojana" is a scholarship scheme that provides financial assistance to girls from economically weaker sections to pursue higher education without any financial burden, and achieve their career goals.

schemeName: Capital Investment Subsidy For Creation Of Infrastructure And Installation Of Permanent Facility/ Acquisition Of Equipment/Tents And Facilities To Establish Adventure Tourism, Water Tourism, Water Sports, Cruise/House Boat, Navigation Infrastructure, Amusement Park, Water Park, Sound And Light Show, Laser Show, Camping (Including Tents)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote adventure and water tourism by financially incentivizing the creation of permanent infrastructure and the acquisition of equipment. It provides a 15% capital subsidy up to ₹3,00,00,000/- to private investors establishing projects such as amusement parks, water sports, etc.

schemeName: Scheme for Construction of Marriage Hall/ Community Hall/ Convention Centre/ Commercial Godown
briefDescription: The scheme aims to assist Qualified Personnel in Medicine, Management, Accountancy, Engineering, Architecture, etc. and Good Borrowers of the Corporation (Industrial Units) for purchase of New Four Wheeler Vehicle (Air Conditioned or Non-air-conditioned) for own use. The applications are online.

schemeName: To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Resources in Tribal Areas: Training Kit - Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide training and equipment to boost inland fisheries among Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries.

schemeName: Sweekruti: Pre & Post Matric Scholarship
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to Transgender students studying at post matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education. These scholarships are available to transgender students  for studies in India only and awarded where the applicant is studying.

schemeName: Investment Promotion Assistance To Setup Large/Mega/Ultra-Mega Tourism Projects
briefDescription: The scheme aims to attract high-value private investment for developing Large, Mega, and Ultra-mega tourism projects in the state. It offers financial assistance of up to 30% of the capital investment to investors who meet minimum investment thresholds and generate employment for Madhya Pradesh.

schemeName: Rehabilitation of Cured Leprosy Persons: Pre & Post-Matric Scholarship
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to the parents of Cured Leprosy Persons in order to support such parents in successfully bringing up their children against societal intolerance, stigma, discrimination and violence.

schemeName: Biju Yuva Sashaktikaran Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance of ₹30,000/-, which is transferred directly to the bank accounts of 15,000 meritorious +2 students through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) for the purchase of a laptop.

schemeName: Technical Education Scholarship Scheme For Diploma Level
briefDescription: This scholarship will be awarded to students who are pursuing Diploma level Courses in Engineering/Technology including Architectural Assistantship or any other equivalent courses as approved by the All India Council of Technical Education (AICTE).

schemeName: Award For Meritorious Students Of Meghalaya In The HSSLC Examination (Science Stream)
briefDescription: The Directorate of Educational Research & Training Meghalaya, Shillong is implementing a scheme in providing a one-time award for meritorious tribal students of Meghalaya who passed the current HSSLC Examination (Science) every year.

schemeName: Dr. Ambedkar Medhavi Chhatravriti Yojna to Other Backward Classes Students
briefDescription: The scheme “Dr. Ambedkar Medhavi Chhatravriti Yojna to Other Backward Classes Students” was started by Dept. of Education, H.P. Under the scheme, scholarship is given to top 1000 meritorious students of OBC declared as such in the result of Matric Examination conducted by H.P. Board.

schemeName: Vidyajyothi Scheme- Financial Aid For Uniforms And Study Materials To PH Students
briefDescription: The scheme “Vidyajyothi Scheme- Financial Aid for Uniforms and Study Materials to PH Students” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

schemeName: Educational Assistance For Children Of Victims Of Crime
briefDescription: The scheme “Educational Assistance for Children of Victims of Crime” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

schemeName: State Talent Search Examination in Mathematics and Science for Tribal Students of Meghalaya at the Close of Upper Primary Stage (Class VIII)
briefDescription: The scheme “State Talent Search Examination in Mathematics and Science for Tribal Students of Meghalaya at the Close of Upper Primary Stage (Class VIII)” was launched by the Education Department, Government of Meghalaya.

schemeName: Financial Assistance to Sportspersons to Pursue Sports Coaching Courses Conducted by SAI, NSNIS, Patiala as a Part of Skill Development Programme to Improve their Employability
briefDescription: The Scheme was launched by the Department of Sports & Youth Services Department, Government of Odisha to provide financial assistance to sportspersons of Odisha to help them become qualified coaches, by undertaking SAI, NSNIS coaching courses, so as to improve their employability.

schemeName: Pratibha Kiran Scholarship
briefDescription: Pratibha Kiran Scholarship has been launched by the Government of Madhya Pradesh. Through this scheme, scholarship will be provided every year to girl students living below the poverty line.

schemeName: The Meghalaya Tribal Students’ Book Grant (Post-Matric Education)
briefDescription: "The Meghalaya Tribal Students’ Book Grant (Post-Matric Education)" scheme was launched by the Directorate of Higher and Technical Education, Meghalaya. Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided to the meritorious tribal students for the purchase of books for their studies.

schemeName: Financial Assistance To Disabled Students Pursuing (10th, 11th, 12th Equivalent Exams)
briefDescription: The scheme “Financial Assistance to Disabled Students Pursuing (10th, 11th, 12th Equivalent Exams)” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

schemeName: Post-Graduate Scholarships for Professional Courses for SC/ST Candidates
briefDescription: The scheme has been initiated to provide opportunity to deprived sections of the society, viz. SC/ST for undertaking postgraduate level studies in Professional subjects like Engineering & Technology, Management, Pharmacy etc. in recognised Indian Universities/Institutions/Colleges.

schemeName: CM's Jibon Anuprerana Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide one-time financial assistance to eligible research scholars from public institutions in Assam, promoting academic excellence and innovation. It provides ₹25,000/- to research scholars and ₹40,000/- to Divyang research scholars.

schemeName: Pradhan Mantri Uchchatar Shiksha Protsahan (PM-USP) Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University students
briefDescription: A scholarship scheme by the Dept. of Higher Education to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from poor families to meet a part of their day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies. The scholarships are awarded on the basis of results of Higher Secondary / Class 12th Board Exam

schemeName: Free Education For Sports Medal Winners / Participants Of National/ International Events
briefDescription: The scheme of "Free Education for Sports Medal Winners / Participants of National/ International Events" is introduced by UGC, to provide financial assistance to the medal winners\ elite sports persons studying in universities/colleges leading to all the full-time regular courses.

nodalMinistryName: NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India)
schemeName: NITI Internship Scheme
briefDescription: This Scheme seeks to engage students pursuing Under Graduate/Graduate/Post Graduate Degrees or are Research Scholars enrolled in recognized University/Institution within India or abroad, as "interns". They shall be given exposure to various Verticals/ Divisions/Units within NITI Aayog.

schemeName: Merit Cum Means Linked Financial Assistance Scheme
briefDescription: It is the policy of the Government of National Capital Territory of Delhi that students get access to high quality higher education provided by Delhi State Universities irrespective of their caste, creed, economic background, or gender.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Unorganized Workers
briefDescription: The scheme “Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa-Unorganized Workers" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. The objective of this scheme is to provide financial benefits to Unorganized Workers (home-based, self-employed or wage workers).

schemeName: Madhya Pradesh Kalakar Evam Sahityakar Kalyan Kosh Niyam- Death Assistance
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide one-time financial assistance to the dependents of deceased artists and litterateurs.

schemeName: Krishi Vidya Nidhi Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide merit-based financial assistance to children of CM-KISAN beneficiaries pursuing professional and technical courses, supporting higher education through Direct Benefit Transfer.

schemeName: Scheduled Tribe Dashamottar Scholarship (Starting from Class 11) - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support Scheduled Tribe students of Uttarakhand from Class 11th onwards by providing financial assistance for their education, including tuition fees and other academic expenses.

schemeName: Scheduled Tribe Purvadasham Scholarship Scheme (Class 1 to 8) - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The aim of this scheme is to help Scheduled Tribe students studying in Classes 1st to 8th by giving them financial support so they can continue their education without any financial difficulty.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Statistics and Programme Implementation
schemeName: Sukhatme National Award in Statistics
briefDescription: The scheme aims to recognise excellence in statistical research and official statistics. Through this scheme, national recognition is provided to eligible Indian statisticians for lifetime or young achiever contributions.

schemeName: Study in India Programme
briefDescription: The Study in India Programme aims to promote India as a global education hub by offering centralized admissions, quality education, and visa support to international students.”

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Dr Ambedkar Central Sector Scheme of Interest Subsidy on Educational Loans for Overseas Studies for Other Backward Classes (OBCs) and Economically Backward Classes (EBCs)
briefDescription: The scheme provides interest subsidy to the students belonging to the OBCs and EBCs on the interest payable for the period of the moratorium for the Education Loans for overseas studies to pursue approved courses of studies abroad at Masters, M.Phil. and PhD level.

schemeName: Special Incentive to OBC Meritorious Students (Boys & Girls)
briefDescription: This scheme offers a financial incentive of up to ₹10,000/- to meritorious Other Backward Classes (OBC) students who have achieved 80% or higher marks in the HSSLC examination. The objective is to motivate and encourage OBC students to pursue higher education.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemeName: Khadi Gramodyog Vikas Yojana: Khadi Vikas Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to increase productivity and wages of Khadi artisans, improve production infrastructure, boost employment and sales, revive traditional rural skills, modernize sales outlets, and promote marketing and exports.

schemeName: Banglar Yuba Sathi
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide monthly financial assistance to educated unemployed youth of West Bengal to support them until they get employment or join another scheme. The objective is to ensure income support for eligible unemployed youth for a maximum period of 5 years.

schemeName: Youth Engagement through Empowered Youth Organisations (YESS)
briefDescription: The objective of this scheme is to empower young people and youth organisations by providing financial support for development activities. It aims to encourage youth participation in community service, sports, cultural events, and leadership initiatives across Meghalaya.

schemeName: A Program to Provide 90 Percent Assistance in Planting Material of Horticultural Fruit Crops
briefDescription: This scheme offers 90% financial assistance to farmers for purchasing planting materials of fruit crops to promote horticulture.

schemeName: Awarding Laptops To Meritorious Students Of Meghalaya
briefDescription: The aims to award laptops to meritorious students of Board Examination. Through this scheme, laptops are provided to eligible students who meet the published merit criteria.

schemeName: Mahila Adhikarita Yojana under NSKFDC- Haryana
briefDescription: Under this scheme, loan is provided to woman Safai Karamchari and their dependent daughters for projects with a maximum project cost of up to ₹2,00,000/- for small and petty trade/businesses, and various income-generating activities such as beauty parlors, boutiques, cosmetic shop, bangle shop, etc.

schemeName: Prime Tourism Vehicle Scheme-Meghalaya
briefDescription: The scheme boosts tourism by providing 50% financial assistance for adventure tourism vehicles, along with easy credit, operator training, and GPS and branding support.

schemeName: Scheme for the Award of Scholarship for Post Graduate Students of Various Faculties of Junagadh Agricultural University
briefDescription: The "Scheme for the Award of Scholarship for Post Graduate Students of Various Faculties of Junagadh Agricultural University" provides monthly scholarships to students pursuing Post Graduate in various faculties at Junagadh Agricultural University.

schemeName: Assistance for Planting Vegetable Crops for Raw and Ripe Mandap
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance to farmers in Gujarat for setting up vegetable trellises (Mandap) for raw, semi-ripe, and ripe vegetables, with varying support for General, SC, and ST farmers.

schemeName: Passenger Auto Rickshaw Loan Scheme (DAADC)
briefDescription: The "Passenger Auto Rickshaw Loan Scheme" introduced by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department offers financial assistance in the form of loans to Scheduled Caste individuals from Antyodaya families to purchase passenger auto rickshaws and enhance their livelihood opportunities.

schemeName: Loan for Training of Commercial Pilot (GUEEDC)
briefDescription: The scheme "Loan for Training of Commercial Pilot (GUEEDC)" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Gujarat, provides financial loan assistance of up to ₹25,00,000/- at 4% simple interest per annum is provided to Unreserved category candidates for pursuing commercial pilot training.

schemeName: Saraswati Sadhana Yojana to Economically Backward Class Girls studying in Class 9
briefDescription: Under this scheme, free bicycle assistance is provided to economically backward class girls studying in Class 9 in government and grant-in-aid schools across Gujarat.

schemeName: Pandit Dindayal Upadhyay Awas Yojana-Developing Caste Welfare
briefDescription: The "Pandit Dindayal Upadhyay Awas Yojana-Developing Caste Welfare" provides financial assistance of up to ₹1,20,000/- to socially and educationally backward classes, economically backward classes, and nomadic exempted castes for constructing houses across Gujarat.

schemeName: Swayam Saksham Yojana (GBCDC)
briefDescription: This scheme aims to instill a sense of self-reliance in the youth belonging to backward classes after receiving vocational education/training. Under this scheme, eligible youth are provided loans at a low-interest rate to start self-employment ventures.

schemeName: Uniform Assistance for Socially and Educationally Backward Class Students Studying Standard 1st to 8th
briefDescription: The scheme "Uniform Assistance for Socially and Educationally Backward Class Students Studying Standard 1st to 8th" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Gujarat, aims to promote educational inclusivity among SEBC students by providing financial assistance of ₹900/- for uniforms.

schemeName: Scholarship for Self Finance Colleges Students of NT/DNT
briefDescription: The scheme "Scholarship for Self Finance Colleges Students of NT/DNT" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat, provides financial assistance to Nomadic and De-notified Tribe students for pursuing higher education in self-financed colleges.

schemeName: Stipends to Students of Economically Backward Class for Technical and Vocational Courses after SSC
briefDescription: The scheme “Stipends to Students of Economically Backward Class for Technical and Vocational Courses after SSC” by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat, supports education for backward class students by providing a monthly stipend for one year.

schemeName: Tuition Sahay Yojana (Unreserved Category)
briefDescription: The scheme "Tution Sahay Yojana (Unreserved Category)" by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Gujarat, aims to provide financial assistance of ₹15,000/- annually to unreserved category students in the science stream of Class 11th and 12th.

schemeName: Micro Finance Scheme for Self Help Groups (GTKVN)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to economically empower women from Thakor and Koli castes through self-employment opportunities via Self Help Groups (SHGs). Under this scheme, eligible members of SHGs receive a loan up to ₹60,000/- at low interest to start or expand small-scale businesses/enterprises.

schemeName: Coaching Assistance for Pre-preparation for Competitive Exams of ALL INDIA level like IIM, CEPT, NIFT, NLU and IELTS, TOFEL, GRE for going Foreign
briefDescription: The scheme aims to assist SEBC and economically backward students in coaching for premier Indian and international exams, the eligible candidates receive up to ₹20,000/- or the actual fee paid.

schemeName: Term Loan Scheme (GSKVN)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to uplift scavengers & their dependents by offering term loans of ₹1,00,000/- to ₹15,00,000/- to support them in starting self‐employment ventures in various trades & professions, enabling them to pursue income‐generating activities and lead dignified lives.

schemeName: Loan for Small Businesses (DAADC)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote self-employment among Scheduled Caste individuals belonging to Antyodaya Anna Yojana families by providing financial assistance for starting small businesses. Under this scheme, eligible beneficiaries are provided a loan of upto ₹1,25,000/- at a concessional interest rate.

schemeName: Mahila Samruddhi Yojana (DAADC)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote self-employment and financial independence among women from the Antyodaya Samaj, especially from Scheduled Castes. Under this scheme, loan up to ₹50,000/- is provided at a concessional interest rate to eligible women for starting small-scale businesses.

schemeName: Mamasaheb Phadke Management of Govt Adarsh ​​Residential Schools (SC) Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to expand access to quality residential education for scheduled caste students in standards 9 to 12. Through this scheme, eligible SC students receive free boarding, lodging, textbooks, uniforms, meals, and additional support services to enhance academic participation.

schemeName: Swami Vivekananda Utkrisht Chhatravriti Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme “Swami Vivekananda Utkrisht Chhatravriti Yojana” is a Scholarship Scheme by the Dept. of Education, Govt. of Himachal Pradesh. An amount of ₹ 18,000/- per year will be given to the general category students for two successive years after matric. 2000 scholarships are awarded every year.

schemeName: Interest Subsidy Scheme on Education Loan
briefDescription: The scheme "Interest Subsidy Scheme on Education Loan" by the Education Department, Gujarat, aims to provide financial support to students by offering 100% interest subsidy on education loans.

schemeName: Meghalaya Sericulture & Weaving Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote entrepreneurship in sericulture and weaving by providing financial support. It also focuses on strengthening Eri and Muga silk production and promoting Meghalaya’s yarn and fabric in national and global markets.

schemeName: Loan For Foreign Study (S.E.B.C.)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support higher education abroad for eligible students from the Socially and Educationally Backward Class (SEBC). It provides a maximum Foreign Study Loan of ₹15,00,000/- at an annual interest rate of 4% to SEBC students.

schemeName: Financial Loan for Training of Commercial Pilot
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support students from Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) in obtaining Commercial Pilot License (CPL) training through financial assistance. Under this scheme, a loan of up to ₹25,00,000 is offered at a concessional interest rate of 4% per annum to the students.....

schemeName: Satfera Samuhlagna (DCW) Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage community marriages among economically and socially backward classes. Incentives are provided to couples and the organizing institutions.

schemeName: Financial Assistance to Helpless Artists
briefDescription: Under this scheme, monthly financial assistance is given to artists and their dependents who are physically disabled, over 60 years old, have a monthly income of less than ₹600, and are unable to sustain themselves due to age or disability.

schemeName: Assistance to Women for Trainer and Coaches Training Course
briefDescription: The scheme “Assistance to Women for Trainer and Coaches Training Course” by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat aims to encourage women's participation in sports by providing financial assistance for diploma courses in sports education.

schemeName: Scheme of Financial Assistance to Literary/Educational Institutions for Organizing Programs/Conferences
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage intellectual and cultural activities by providing financial support to institutions conducting seminars, workshops, symposiums, and literary programs.

schemeName: Personality Development and Yogasan Training Camp for Schedule Caste
briefDescription: The scheme "Personality Development and Yogasan Training Camp for Schedule Caste Youth" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to promote the physical, mental, and spiritual development of Scheduled Caste youth through 7-day training camps.

schemeName: PRISM (Promoting Innovations in Individuals, Start-ups and MSMEs): Category-2: Fabrication of Working Model/Process Know-how/Testing & Trial/Patenting/Technology Transfer
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote innovations in individuals, start-ups, and Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (PRISM). It offers financial support (up to ₹20,00,000/-) for Indian Citizens working on innovation projects requiring fabrication of working models, patenting, or technology transfer.

schemeName: Teachers Associateship for Research Excellence
briefDescription: The scheme “Teachers Associateship for Research Excellence (TARE)” was launched by Science & Engineering Research Board, Department of Science & Technology, Government of India which aims to facilitate mobility of faculty members working in State Universities /Colleges/Private Academic institutions.

schemeName: Raman-Charpak Fellowship
briefDescription: The “Raman-Charpak Fellowship Programme” is in honour of two Nobel Laureates in Physics, Prof C.V. Raman, Indian Nobel Laureate (1930) & Prof Georges Charpak, French Nobel Laureate (1992). The Fellowship was launched during the State visit of the President of France to India in February, 2013.

schemeName: One District One Product (ODOP) Awards
briefDescription: The scheme aims to recognize and celebrate the outstanding efforts of States, UTs, Districts, and Indian Missions Abroad in promoting local products. It also seeks to boost economic development and support the Hon’ble Prime Minister’s vision of Atmanirbhar Bharat.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: Rashtriya Krishi Vigyan Puraskar
briefDescription: The Rashtriya Krishi Vigyan Puraskar (RKVP) aims to honor scientists and technical experts for outstanding contributions and innovations in agriculture and allied sciences.

schemeName: Police Medal for Meritorious Service
briefDescription: Under this scheme, medals, official recognition, and service distinction are provided to the personnel serving in police, CAPFs, CPOs, and other public safety organizations.

schemeName: The Lokpal of India Internship Scheme
briefDescription: The Lokpal of India Internship Scheme offers students exposure to anti-corruption laws and institutions. Interns learn about governance, legal frameworks, and contribute ideas to enhance transparency and accountability in public administration.

schemeName: Old Age Pension To Handloom Weavers Under Coop. Fold
briefDescription: The “Old Age Pension To Handloom Weavers” scheme implemented by the MSME and Textiles Dept., aims to provide monthly financial assistance in the form of pension to a weaver member of a handloom cooperative society.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Skill Development And Entrepreneurship
schemeName: National Award to Teachers (For Excellence in Vocational Training and Entrepreneurship Development)
briefDescription: The objective of this scheme is to recognize and honor excellence in vocational training and entrepreneurship development by faculty and trainers.

schemeName: Padma Awards
briefDescription: The scheme aims to recognize distinguished and exceptional achievements across various fields of public service and national importance.

schemeName: Halpati Housing Scheme
briefDescription: Under this scheme, ₹1,20,000 financial assistance is provided for self-constructed houses in rural south Gujarat to support housing needs of Halpati (Talaviya, Nayaka) caste members included in Scheduled Tribes.

schemeName: Meghalaya Motorcaravan Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme promotes alternative tourism and supports rural and eco-tourism in the state. It encourages entrepreneurship by providing financial assistance for operating motorcaravans and aims to create employment opportunities while offering unique travel experiences across Meghalaya.

schemeName: Agro Tourism Villa Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote high-value tourism in Meghalaya by encouraging the development of agro-tourism villas, empowering local youth through entrepreneurship, improving accommodation facilities, and preserving the state’s cultural and natural heritage.

schemeName: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Expansion of Coffee
briefDescription: The scheme "Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Expansion of Coffee" by the Coffee Board, Department of Commerce, Ministry of Commerce and Industry, aims to facilitate afforestation in the Jhum lands and help settle the tribals on a permanent footing.

schemeName: Scheme For Grant Of Rewards To The Children Of Salt Labourers
briefDescription: The scheme “Scheme for Grant of Rewards to the Children of Salt Labourers” was launched by Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry in the year 1985.

schemeName: Women Scientist Scheme-A
briefDescription: A Research Grant by MoST for female candidates to pursue research in frontier areas of science and engineering, preferably those having a break in career (minimum two years) and not having regular employment, to explore possibility of re-entry into the profession.

schemeName: Women’s Instinct for Developing and Ushering in Scientific Heights & Innovation
briefDescription: 'WIDUSHI' programme aims to encourage and support senior women scientists to conduct research in interdisciplinary areas of Science and Technology.

schemeName: Mobility Scheme
briefDescription: The Mobility Scheme is aimed to provide an opportunity to women scientists who are facing difficulties in their present job due to relocation (marriage, transfer of husband to any other location within the country, attending ailing parents, and accompanying children studying in different city).

schemeName: Post Graduate Indira Gandhi Scholarship For Single Girl Child
briefDescription: A scholarship scheme by the University Grants Commission for Girl students who are admitted in the first year of PG courses in any designated university or a postgraduate college, and happen to be the only girl child in the family. The amount of fellowship is ₹ 36,200/- per annum.

schemeName: Post Matric Scholarship To Scheduled Tribe Students Of Nagaland
briefDescription: The Directorate of Higher Education (DHE) has launched the Post Matric Scholarship to ST students of the Nagaland scheme for providing socio-economic welfare to the students belonging to the residentship of Nagaland state. The student must be studying or getting admission to the post-matric class.

schemeName: National Savings Time Deposit (TD)
briefDescription: The objective of the National Savings Time Deposit (TD) scheme is to promote safe and fixed-term savings by offering assured returns, encouraging individuals to invest regularly with flexible deposit amounts and no upper limit.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Tagore Cultural Complexes (TCC)
briefDescription: This scheme promotes cultural activities across states and fosters unity while providing opportunities for creative expression and learning, especially for the youth.

schemeName: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Power Tiller
briefDescription: The scheme offers loans to farmers covering up to 90% of the cost of a power tiller, with an interest rate between 11% and 12.25%.

schemeName: Special Incentive to ST (P)/OBC/MOBC Meritorious Students
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial grants to meritorious ST (P)/OBC/MOBC students in Assam who secure high marks in Matriculation, 10+2, Graduation, or Post Graduation. Benefits include fixed monetary assistance ranging from ₹10,000/- to ₹25,000/- based on educational level.

schemeName: Agnipath Yojana
briefDescription: A welfare scheme by the Department of Military Affairs, MoD under which rigorous military training will be imparted to the youth aged between 17.5 to 23 years and meeting other educational, physical, and medical criteria, who are aspirational to be a part of the armed forces.

schemeName: Pradhan Mantri Virasat ka Samvardhan
briefDescription: PM VIKAS is a Central sector scheme that focuses on socio-economic empowerment of six notified minorities in the country through skilling & training, women leadership & empowerment and education. The scheme integrates erstwhile skilling & education schemes of the Ministry of Minority Affairs.

beneficiaryState: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
schemeName: Pension Scheme to Widow- DNH & DD
briefDescription: The scheme is implemented by the Social Welfare Department, aims to provide financial assistance to widows aged between 18-59 years. The scheme offers ₹1,000/- per month to help meet their basic needs, ensuring public assistance for widows in need of support to maintain a dignified life.

schemeName: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide social security pensions to divyangjans aged 18 and above who belong to a poor family (BPL) in society.

schemeName: Market Development Assistance
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote organic fertilizers produced at Bio-Gas and Compressed Bio-Gas plants under the Galvanizing Organic Bio-Agro Resources Dhan initiative. It provides Market Development Assistance of ₹1,500/- per Metric Tonne to manufacturing and marketing entities of organic fertilizers.

schemeName: National Post Doctoral Fellowship
briefDescription: The “National Post Doctoral Fellowship (N-PDF)” was launched by the Science and Engineering Research Board, Government of India to identify motivated young researchers and provide them support for doing research in frontier areas of science and engineering.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Personnel, Public Grievances And Pensions
schemeName: Right To Information Fellowship
briefDescription: Under this scheme, DOPT proposes to offer four short-term fellowships to researchers from the field of media / Civil Society professionals / RTI Trainers to conduct field-based research on themes relating to RTI. It is hoped that the research output will enhance their understanding of the RTI Act.

schemeName: Biotechnology Research Innovation And Entrepreneurship Development (Bio-RIDE)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to foster innovation, promote bio-entrepreneurship, and strengthen India's position as a global leader in biomanufacturing and biotechnology. It provides seed funding, extramural grants, and infrastructure support to startups, enterprises, industries, and academia.

schemeName: Integrated Scheme for Export Promotion & Quality Improvement in Spices and Research and Development of Cardamom: \Small Cardamom Replanting/New Planting: Karnataka (Ha.) and other States
briefDescription: To motivate growers in Karnataka and other states to improve production and productivity through systematic replanting of diseased, old, and senile plantations, and to promote area expansion under cultivation.

schemeName: Innovation In Science Pursuit For Inspired Research (INSPIRE) - Fellowship
briefDescription: INSPIRE Fellowship is one of the components of INSPIRE scheme implemented by the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India to strengthen the Science and Technology base of the country. This Component focuses on attracting students to pursue doctoral degree.

schemeName: Marketing Development Assistance Scheme
briefDescription: The Marketing Development Assistance (MDA) Scheme under the Overseas Promotion & Publicity (OPP) Scheme aims to support the promotion and marketing of Indian tourism in overseas markets.

schemeName: Biotechnology Ignition Grant Scheme
briefDescription: The “Biotechnology Ignition Grant Scheme (BIG)” is a flagship scheme of BIRAC which recognizes and invests in innovative ideas in the biotech sector. BIG scheme supports individual entrepreneurs/ start-ups from ideation to Proof of Concept.

schemeName: PM POSHAN - Prime Minister's Overarching Scheme For Holistic Nourishment
briefDescription: PM POSHAN is a centrally sponsored scheme by the Department of School Education & Literacy, Ministry of Education. Under this scheme, one hot cooked meal will be provided to the children studying in Government and Government – aided schools. The Scheme is implemented by the Ministry of Education.

schemeName: Jute Diversification Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to set up production centres to promote eco-friendly jute products and generate sustained employment. It provides skill training, machine subsidies, and financial incentives to collaborating agencies, targeting artisans, women self-help groups, and entrepreneurs.

schemeName: Micro Finance Scheme of NMDFC
briefDescription: Under the scheme, an amount of Rs. 1 lakh under credit Line-1 & upto  Rs. 1.5 lakh under Credit Line-2 is extended to each member of SHG (belong to Minority communities) at interest rate of 7% & 10% respectively.  Concession of 2% is extended to women beneficiaries under Credit Line-2.

schemeName: Production Linked Incentive Support on JDPs
briefDescription: The scheme aims to make Indian Jute Diversified Products globally cost-competitive and increase their export market share. It provides a financial reimbursement of up to ₹12,00,000/- per annum on raw material costs for Jute Mill Companies and MSME units engaged in exporting JDPs.

schemeName: Term Loan Scheme of NMDFC
briefDescription: Under this scheme, credit of up to ₹20,00,000/- under Credit Line-1 and up to ₹30,00,000/- under Credit line -2 is extended to beneficiaries among minority communities at 6%  p.a. & 8% p.a. respectively for financing economically viable income generation ventures.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Scheduled Caste Students
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support the education of Scheduled Caste students at the pre-matric stage, reduce dropouts, and improve educational participation. Through this scheme, annual academic allowances are provided to eligible Scheduled Caste students studying in Classes IX and X.

schemeName: National Savings (Monthly Income  Account) Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme provides a fixed monthly income to investors through periodic interest payments on a one-time deposit made at the time of account opening.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: RKVY Soil Health and Fertility - Soil Health Card
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote soil test-based nutrient management by providing Soil Health Cards containing soil analysis results and fertilizer recommendations to farmers across India.

schemeName: Hari Om Ashram Prerit Doctor Vikram Sarabhai Research Award
briefDescription: The Shri Hari Om Ashram Prerit Doctor Vikram Sarabhai Research Awards are biennial awards established with funds from Pujya Shri Mota of Hari Om Ashram, Nadiad, to recognize outstanding contributions by Indian scientists under the age of 45 in the fields of Space Sciences etc.

beneficiaryState: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
schemeName: Unique Disability Identity - DNH & DD
briefDescription: UDID Card or Unique Disability Identity card is an initiative by Government of India with a view of creating a National Database for Persons with Disabilities (PwDs). Under the UDID project Disability Certificate and Unique Disability Identity Card is issued to each PwD through online platform.

beneficiaryState: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
schemeShortTitle: SUPPLY OF STATIONERY, SOCKS, SHOES, SCHOOL BAG
schemeName: Supply of School Stationery, Socks, Shoes, School Bag, etc. to all Students of Std I to VIII
briefDescription: Supply of School Stationery, Socks, Shoes, School Bag, etc. to all Students of Std. I to VIII studying in Govt Schools

beneficiaryState: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
schemeName: Supply of Textbook to all Students for IX to XII
briefDescription: Supply of Textbook to all Students for IX to XII studying in Govt Schools

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Post Matric Scholarship Students With Disabilities
briefDescription: A scholarship scheme by DoEPwD for students with disabilities pursuing a post-matric qualification from UGC/AICTE recognized universities, i.e. students of Class 11th, Class 12th, including Polytechnics, Post-Matriculation Diploma/Certificates and Bachelor’s/Master’s Degree/Diploma in India.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Top Class Education For Scheduled Caste Students
briefDescription: The Scheme aims at recognising and promoting quality education amongst students belonging to SCs by providing financial support. The Scheme will cover SC students for pursuing studies beyond the 12th class.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: National Talent Scholarship Undergraduate
briefDescription: The scholarship aims to support meritorious students by providing financial assistance of ₹3,000 per month during their undergraduate studies.

schemeName: AICTE - Distinguished Chair Professor Fellowship
briefDescription: The fellowship intends to utilize the expertise of highly qualified and experienced superannuated professionals who have made an unparalleled, exceptional professional contribution to society in their respective fields/disciplines.

schemeName: Women Scientist Scheme-B
briefDescription: A Research Grant by MoST for female candidates with break in their career interested in addressing societal challenges through viable S&T interventions. Women scientists who wish to apply under this scheme are required to develop their own project/proposals for scientific and technological solutions

schemeName: NPS Vatsalya Scheme
briefDescription: NPS Vatsalya is a contributory saving cum long-term financial security scheme designed exclusively for minors for the purposes of enhancing financial literacy and financial planning and to encourage empowerment of children, emanating from the vision of "Viksit Bharat@2047.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: National Food Security Mission NFSM
briefDescription: National Food Security Mission was launched in 2007-08 to increase the production of rice, wheat and pulses. Later, coarse cereals, nutri cereals, commercial crops (sugarcane, jute, and cotton) were also added to the mission.

schemeName: Annapurna Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme “Annapurna Yojana” implemented by the Women and Child Development and Social Welfare Department, Government of West Bengal aims to provide assured monthly financial assistance to eligible women residing in the state for their empowerment and socio-economic upliftment. Under the scheme, el

schemeName: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan
briefDescription: The scheme aims to ensure old age protection for unorganised workers. It provides a minimum assured pension of ₹3,000/- per month and family pension benefits to unorganised workers aged 18 to 40 years who earn less than ₹15,000/- monthly, supported by matching government contributions.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: NBCFDC General Loan Scheme
briefDescription: A general loan scheme by the Ministry of Social Justice for Indian citizens from backward classes for various income-generating activities. Loan up to ₹15 Lakh per beneficiary for pursuing income-generating activities.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: Kisan Credit Card
briefDescription: The Kisan Credit Card (KCC) scheme was introduced in 1998 for issue of Kisan Credit Cards to farmers on the basis of their holdings for uniform adoption by the banks so that farmers may use them to readily purchase agriculture inputs and draw cash for their production needs.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Pre Matric Scholarship For Students With Disabilities
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support students with disabilities for further studies and reduce dropout rates. Through this scheme, maintenance allowance, book grant, and disability allowance are provided to eligible students with disabilities studying in Classes IX and X.

schemeName: Pragati Scholarship Scheme For Girl Students (Technical Diploma)
briefDescription: A scholarship scheme by AICTE for Girl Students admitted in 1st or 2nd Year of the technical diploma course in an AICTE approved institution. The benefits are provided for a period of max. of 3 years for first-year students and a max. of 2 years for second-year admitted students (later entry).

schemeName: National Youth Corps
briefDescription: A volunteership scheme by Ministry of Youth Affairs And Sports to tap the potential of the disciplined and dedicated youth In the age group of 18 to 29 years who have the inclination and spirit to engage in nation building exercises, to serve up to two years in Nation Building activities.

schemeName: SVAMITVA Yojana (Survey of Villages and Mapping with Improvised Technology in Village Areas)
briefDescription: SVAMITVA, a Central Sector scheme of Ministry of Panchayati Raj to provide ‘Record of Rights’ to village household owners with issuance of legal ownership cards (Property cards/Title deeds) to the property owners by mapping of land parcels using drone technology.

schemeName: Atal Pension Yojana
briefDescription: Atal Pension Yojana (APY) is an old age income security scheme for a  savings account holder  in the age group of 18-40 years,  who is not an income tax-payee. It helps to address longevity risks among  workers in unorganized sector and encourages the workers to voluntarily save for retirement.

schemeName: Health Minister’s Discretionary Grant
briefDescription: A health scheme by Ministry of Health & Family Welfare for financially poor patients to defray a part of the expenditure on Hospitalization/treatment in Government Hospitals,for life threatening diseases covered under Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi (RAN), in cases where free medical facilities aren't there.

schemeName: Swami Vivekananda Single Girl Child Fellowship for Research in Social Sciences
briefDescription: A fellowship scheme by the University Grants Commission (UGC) for Single Girl Child for research in Social Sciences with an aim to compensate for direct costs of higher education, especially for such girls who happen to be the only girl child in their family.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Fisheries,Animal Husbandry and Dairying
schemeName: National Scheme Of Welfare Of Fishermen
briefDescription: The National Scheme of Welfare of Fishermen provides financial assistance to fishermen. They can use these for constructing houses and community halls for both recreation and work purposes. Further, through the amount availed under this scheme, fishermen can install tube wells.

schemeName: Integrated Social Security Scheme - Women In Distress
briefDescription: ISSS (Integrated Social Security Scheme) is a State Sponsored Scheme by the Social Welfare Department of the Govt. of Jammu and Kashmir where financial assistance is provided to Old Age Persons, Widows, Divorcees, Women in Distress, Transgenders, and Physically Handicapped Persons.

schemeName: Integrated Social Security Scheme - Pension For Physically Challenged Persons
briefDescription: ISSS-PPCP is a State Sponsored Scheme by the Social Welfare Dept. of the Govt. of J&K wherein financial assistance is provided to Physically Challenged Person who have meager support or no source of livelihood. A pension of ₹1000 per pensioner per month is deposited into their bank account.

schemeName: Savitribai Phule Scholarship For V.J.N.T. And S.B.C. Girl Students Studying In 5th To 7th Standard
briefDescription: Savitribai Phule Scholarship is a Scholarship Scheme by the Social Justice and Special Assistance Dept., Govt. of Maharashtra, to encourage the enrollment and to decrease the dropout ratio of Girl Students of Maharashtra from Vimukta Jati and Nomadic Tribes and Special Backward Classes.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: Krishonnati Yojana - Sub Mission On Seed And Planting Material (SMSP)
briefDescription: The Sub-Mission on Seeds and Planting Material (SMSP) aims to increase the production of certified / quality seed, increase SRR, upgrade the quality of farm-saved seeds, strengthen the seed multiplication chain, promote new technologies and methodologies in seed production, processing, testing, etc.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Baristha Bunakar and Karigara Sahayata Yojana
briefDescription: Mukhyamantri Baristha Bunakar and Karigara Sahayata Yojana is a welfare scheme initiated by the Handlooms, Textiles and Handicrafts Department, Government of Odisha to provide financial assistance to elderly individuals involved in weaving and craft work, who may face economic difficulties.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Bayan Jyoti Yojana
briefDescription: The Scheme will be implemented "Mukhyarrantri Bayan Jyoti Yojana" (MBJY) under Chief Minister's Special Package for Handloom weavers.

schemeName: Lakshmir Bhandar Scheme
briefDescription: The "Lakshmir Bhandar" scheme is a flagship program to provide financial assistance to women from economically weaker sections of society, for the empowerment of women in the age group of 25-60 years, and enrolled in ‘Swasthya Sathi’.

schemeName: Pension To The Disabled Person
briefDescription: The objective of the pension to the disabled person scheme is providing a pension to disabled persons is multifaceted, but generally centers around promoting social welfare, equity, and inclusivity.

schemeName: Chief Minister Samagra Gramya Unnayan Yojana
briefDescription: A 5-year mega-mission called Chief Minister Samagra Gramya Unnayan Yojana (CMSGUY) was launched in the Financial Year(FY) 2016-17, which is scheduled to culminate in the FY 2021-22, coinciding with 75 years of India's Independence.

schemeName: Niramaya Scheme
briefDescription: The Department of Health & Family Welfare Department, Government of Odisha introduced a scheme named "NIRAMAYA" - a Free Medicines Distribution Scheme. The aim is to provide quality essential medicines for all kinds of diseases to patients visiting government health facilities at free of cost.

schemeName: Assistance Scheme For Reimbursement For Expenditure On Visa By Construction Workers For Employment Abroad
briefDescription: This scheme will be called "Reimbursement of expenditure incurred on visa for employment abroad to construction workers".
