schemeName: Gopabandhu Sikhya Sahayata Yojana
briefDescription: The "Gopabandhu Sikhya Sahayata Yojana" scheme was launched as an endeavor to make higher education inclusive and to increase the gross enrollment ratio. The scheme aims to further widen access to higher education for students belonging to underprivileged sections of society.

schemeName: Mukhya Mantri Gyandeep Yojna
briefDescription: A scheme to provide financial support in the form of interest subsidy on Educational Loans availed from Banks by Himachali Students for pursuing Higher Education in India and Abroad.

schemeName: Thakur Sen Negi Utkrisht Chatravriti Yojana
briefDescription: The objective of this scheme is to provide scholarships of ₹11,000/-per annum to the top 100 Girls and top 100 Boys students belonging to the Tribal Community of Himachal Pradesh who have passed Class 10th and presently studying in a recognized institution within or outside the State.

schemeName: Distance Education Scholarship–Scholarship For Disabled Students Pursuing Degree, PG Courses
briefDescription: The scheme “Distance Education Scholarship–Scholarship for Disabled Students Pursuing Degree, PG Courses” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

schemeName: Financial Assistance To Visually Impaired Advocates
briefDescription: The scheme “Financial Assistance to visually impaired Advocates” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

schemeName: Scheme for Promoting Young Talents in Science-II
briefDescription: The scheme “Scheme for Promoting Young Talents in Science-II (SPYTiS-II)” is constituted for the purpose of providing financial and technical assistance to talented young students who have innovative ideas in scientific/technological pursuits.

schemeName: Snehasanthwanam
briefDescription: The Government of Kerala has launched the “Snehasanthwanam” scheme under the Kerala Social Security Mission. The scheme acts as a relief measure for the Endosulfan victims in the state of Kerala.

schemeName: NEC Stipend And Book Grant Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote national integration, global understanding, social responsibility, and encourage scientific, imaginative, and innovative thinking among students.

schemeName: Snehapoorvam Scheme
briefDescription: Kerala Government has launched a noble initiative “Snehapoorvam Scheme” to provide financial assistance to orphans who are living in the family, with their relatives, friends, or the support of the community under the Social Welfare Department implemented through Social Security Mission.

schemeName: Post Graduate Scholarship Scheme for GATE/GPAT Qualified Students-AICTE
briefDescription: AICTE in order to ensure the development of technical education in India awards Post Graduate Scholarship of ₹12,400/- per month to full-time GATE/CEED qualified students admitted to AICTE approved post-graduate programs in AICTE approved Institutions/University Dept. as per AICTE approved intake.

schemeName: Attendance Scholarship Scheme for Girl Students with Disabilities of Rural Areas
briefDescription: The objective of this scheme is to uplift the status of the girls with disabilities in the rural areas & also to make them reliant by paying incentives in the form of Attendance Scholarship to these girls.

schemeName: Stipend Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
briefDescription: The "Stipend Scheme'' scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides financial support as stipend for the education of children of registered construction workers, including hostel fees, and extends to evening classes for the workers themselves.

schemeName: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar State Award To SC/ST/OBC/Minorities Students
briefDescription: Objective of the Scheme to recognise and reward meritorious SC/ST/OBC/Minority students pursuing graduation in recognised institutions.

schemeName: Post Matric Scholarship for Students belonging to Scheduled Caste Community
briefDescription: The scheme "Post Matric Scholarship for Students belonging to Scheduled Caste Community" was launched by the Mizoram Scholarship Board, Government of Mizoram. The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to students belonging to Scheduled Castes pursuing post-matriculation.

schemeName: Combined Merit Scholarship
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide merit-based scholarship to students of Assam studying in first semester degree and master degree courses in recognized institutions. It benefits academically meritorious students who have obtained at least 60% marks in their last qualifying examination.

schemeName: Financial Assistance For Purchase Of Stationery To SC/ST/OBC/Minorities Students
briefDescription: Welfare scheme of Department For The Welfare Of SC/ST/OBC’s/Minorities to provide help for purchase of stationery to SC/ST/OBC/Minorities Students of 1st to 12th class studying Govt. of India/GNCT of Delhi/Aided Recognized schools.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Persons with Disabilities
briefDescription: The scheme “Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Persons with Disabilities" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura to provide financial assistance to Persons with Disabilities in the form of a monthly pension of ₹2,000/-.

schemeName: Reimbursement of Tution Fee for SC/ST/OBC/Min students in Public Schools in Class I to XII
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial support by reimbursing tuition and compulsory fees for SC/ST/OBC students from low-income families (below ₹3,00,000/- per annum) studying in recognized schools in Delhi. Beneficiaries must meet academic and attendance criteria.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Old and Infirm
briefDescription: The scheme “Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa- Old and Infirm" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. This scheme aims to provide financial assistance to poor people of Tripura who are old and infirm, such as senior citizens.

beneficiaryState: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
schemeName: Pradhan Mantri Poshan Shakti Nirman - DNH&DD
briefDescription: The PM POSHAN Scheme, implemented by the Department of Education, DNH & DD, provides nutritious mid-day meals to school children. Earlier known as the Mid Day Meal Scheme (launched on 15.08.1995), it was renamed in 2021 to improve nutrition, attendance, and learning outcomes.

schemeName: Chief Minister BC & EBC Chhatravas Anudan Yojana
briefDescription: The "Chief Minister BC & EBC Chhatravas Anudan Yojana", implemented by the Backward Classes and Extremely Backward Classes Welfare Department, provides financial assistance to students from Backward Classes (BC) and Extremely Backward Classes (EBC) residing in government hostels.

schemeName: Scheduled Caste Dashamottar Scholarship (Starting from Class 11) - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support Scheduled Caste students of Uttarakhand from Class 11th onwards by providing financial assistance for their education, including tuition fees and other academic expenses.

schemeName: Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Gratuity Assistance in case of Death
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide social security to registered drivers and conductors by offering gratuity assistance for funeral expenses to families in the event of an accidental death.

schemeName: Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Training and Rehabilitation in Case of Permanent Disability due to Accident
briefDescription: The scheme aims to rehabilitate drivers and conductors who suffer permanent disability due to accidents by providing skill training for alternative employment to the beneficiaries.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Statistics and Programme Implementation
schemeName: Research Study/Seminar (Grant-in-Aid Scheme)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote Research Studies, Seminars, and Travel Grants Assistance in official statistics. It provides financial assistance and data support to eligible research institutions/organizations and Central Government officials to bridge data gaps.

schemeName: Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Sarathi Shri Award
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide one of the state's best driver "Sarathi Shri" award, which includes a cash prize, a certificate of appreciation, and a trophy.

schemeName: Auto Rickshaw Regulation Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to regulate auto rickshaw operations by enforcing standardized permits and compliance conditions statewide to ensure safety and uniformity.

schemeName: Other Backward Caste Dashamottar Scholarship Scheme (Starting from Class 11) - Uttarakhand
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support Other Backward Class (OBC) students of Uttarakhand from Class 11th onwards by providing financial assistance for their education, including tuition fees and other academic expenses.

schemeName: Economically Backward Class (EBC) Dashamottar Scholarship Scheme (Starting from Class 11)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support Economically Backward Class (EBC) students of Uttarakhand from Class 11th onwards by providing financial assistance for their education, including tuition fees and other academic expenses.

schemeName: Assam Tea Plantations Provident Fund Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial security to Assam tea plantation workers through a contributory provident fund. It benefits eligible employees earning up to ₹6,500/- per month by accumulating 12% contributions from both the worker and employer, earning interest to be paid upon retirement.

schemeName: Prani Sampad Samruddhi Yojana: Small Scale Goat / Sheep Farming (10+1 Capacity)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide livelihood support to the interested beneficiaries to improve the livelihoods of the weaker sections of society. Through this scheme, a subsidy of 60% or 50% of the unit cost is provided to eligible beneficiaries for the establishment of one Goat / Sheep Unit (10+1).

schemeName: Meghalaya Piggery Development Scheme
briefDescription: The Meghalaya Piggery Development Scheme is a component of the Chief Minister’s CM-ELEVATE Program launched by the Government of Meghalaya. It aims to employ, engage, and empower youth and rural communities by promoting piggery entrepreneurship through easy access to credit and subsidies.

schemeName: Sukhad Sahara Yojana
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹500/- per month are provided to the widows and deserted women who are living below poverty line (BPL) to support them in living a respectful life.

schemeName: Cluster Development Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to enhance the productivity and competitiveness as well as capacity building of Micro and Small Enterprises through cluster development approach. It provides various assistance including training, subsidies, and marketing facilities to clusters of 25 or more artisans.

schemeName: Crop Input Subsidy to the Farmers for Crop Loss Due to Natural Calamities
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance to farmers in Gujarat who suffer crop losses due to natural calamities, based on crop damage surveys conducted by the district administration.

schemeName: Prime Small Enterprise Empowerment and Development(SEED) Scheme
briefDescription: Under the scheme provides financial support for setting up or expanding small business ventures. It primarily targets the unorganized sector and underserved business activities that contribute significantly to the local economy but have historically lacked institutional support.

schemeName: Assistance for Area Expansion of Spice Crops
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance to farmers for the area expansion of spice crops, including seed and rhizomatic spices, to enhance spice production in Gujarat.

schemeName: Sheep Rearing Scheme (HSFDC)
briefDescription: Under this scheme, the Corporation provides loans/benefits to individuals from Below Poverty Line (BPL) families, whose annual family income does not exceed ₹1,80,000/-, in both rural and urban areas for self-employment income generating activities such as sheep rearing.

schemeName: Assistance Program for Expansion of Fruit Crop Cultivation
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance to farmers in Gujarat for expanding fruit crop cultivation like mango, banana, papaya, etc., through intensive and ultra-intensive farming methods.

schemeName: Planting Assistance Program for Hybrid Seeds of Vegetable Crops
briefDescription: This scheme offers financial assistance to farmers for the plantation of hybrid seeds and promotion of natural farming in vegetable crops across Gujarat.

schemeName: Scheme for the Award of Scholarship for the Under Graduate Meritorious Students of Various Faculties of Junagadh Agricultural University
briefDescription: The "Scheme for the Award of Scholarship for the Under Graduate Meritorious Students of Various Faculties of Junagadh Agricultural University" provides monthly scholarships to students pursuing Under Graduate in various faculties at Junagadh Agricultural University.

schemeName: Capital Subsidy Scheme for Setting Up Solar Plant in Milk Cooperatives in Gujarat State (Tribal Area)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote renewable energy and reduce electricity expenses. Through this scheme, a 75% subsidy is provided for setting up solar plants (1KW to 40KW) in milk cooperative societies in tribal areas.

schemeName: Scheme for Awarding Fellowships for Undergraduate and Postgraduate Students
briefDescription: The "Scheme for Awarding Fellowships for Undergraduate and Postgraduate Students" provides financial support in the form of monthly scholarships to students pursuing higher education in various faculties at Anand Agricultural University.

schemeName: Scheme on Promotion of Use of Liquid Fermented Organic Manure (LFOM) for Increasing Organic Carbon in Soil
briefDescription: The "Scheme on Promotion of Use of Liquid Fermented Organic Manure (LFOM) for Increasing Organic Carbon in Soil" offers financial assistance to promote the use of Liquid Fermented Organic Manure (LFOM) and enhance organic carbon levels in soil.

schemeName: Mukhya Mantri Agricultural Mechanization Scheme
briefDescription: The "Mukhya Mantri Agricultural Mechanization Scheme" launched by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, offers financial assistance ranging from ₹1,200/- to ₹11,00,000/- to farmers in Gujarat for purchasing agricultural machinery and farm equipment.

schemeName: PMMSY: Insulated vehicles - Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to strengthen fish storage infrastructure for hygienic preservation. Through this scheme, beneficiaries receive financial assistance for Insulated vehicles.

schemeName: Micro Finance (GBCDC)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to create self-employment for the backwards class men. Through this scheme, low interest rates and financial help are provided to the backwards class men.

schemeName: Scheme to Provide Electric Scooter to Disabled Person
briefDescription: The "Scheme to Provide Electric Scooter to Disabled Person" provides financial assistance of up to ₹25,000 for the purchase of electric scooters to eligible persons with orthopedic disabilities.

schemeName: Uniform Assistance for Scheduled Tribe Students
briefDescription: The scheme "Uniform Assistance for Scheduled Tribe Students" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Gujarat, provides ₹900/- for three pairs of uniforms to ST students in government and aided primary schools studying in Classes 1 to 8.

schemeName: Scholarship to Disabled Students
briefDescription: The scheme "Scholarship to Disabled Students" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department aims to provide financial assistance to students with disabilities. Through this scheme, annual scholarships are offered to support the education of eligible students from Class 1 onwards.

schemeName: Pashupalan Dhiran Yojana
briefDescription: "Pashupalan Dhiran Yojana" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department provides loan assistance for animal husbandry to Rabari and Bharwad castes from the socially and educationally backward classes.

schemeName: New Akanksha Scheme (Educational Loan) (GTKVN)
briefDescription: Under this scheme, education loans are provided at subsidized interest rates to students belonging to Thakor and Koli communities for pursuing graduate and postgraduate studies in fields such as engineering, medical, MBA, and MCA.

schemeName: Scheme to Encourage the Children of Sanitation Workers Who have Passed the Class 10/12 Examination (GSKVN)
briefDescription: Under this scheme, children of sanitation workers who have achieved first, second, or third rank in the board exams of Class 10 and Class 12 (General Stream and Science Stream) across the state are awarded cash prizes and certificates of appreciation.

schemeName: Pashu Palan Yojana (GSKVN)
briefDescription: Under this scheme, loans of up to ₹1,00,000 are provided to sanitation workers and their dependents at an interest rate of 6% for self-employment in animal husbandry or related activities.

schemeName: Mobile Food Court Van (DAADC)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to generate self-employment opportunities in the food and beverage sector by providing credit to unemployed scheduled caste individuals belonging to Antyodaya families. Under this scheme, loans are provided to the eligible beneficiaries at a concessional interest rate.

schemeName: Food Bill Assistance for Medical & Engineering Students of Nomadic Tribes & Denotified Tribes
briefDescription: The scheme supports students from Nomadic Tribes and Denotified Tribes pursuing full-time Medical or Engineering courses in Gujarat by reducing their living expenses. Eligible students receive ₹1,500/- per month for up to 10 months each academic year to cover mess or canteen expenses.

schemeName: Pashu Palan Yojana (GTKVN)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to enhance the livelihoods of Thakor and Koli communities by providing financial assistance for animal husbandry activities such as dairy farming. Under this scheme, loans of up to ₹1,00,000/- are provided for the purchase of milch animals such as cows and buffaloes.

schemeName: Kumar Rajratna Bhimrao Ambedkar Medical Assistance Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial support to individuals belonging to SC category suffering from incurable & serious diseases. Under this scheme, Medical Assistance is provided to Scheduled Caste patients diagnosed with life-threatening conditions such as TB, Leukemia, & other critical illnesses.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Preparation of Training Camp and Participation in Land, Mountain & Sea Adventure Sports Competitions
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote youth participation in adventure sports by providing financial assistance for training, workshops, and competitions.

schemeName: Private Tuition Assistance to Socially and Educationally Backward Class Students Studying in Science Stream of Class 11 & 12
briefDescription: Under this scheme, a grant of ₹15,000 each for class 11 and class 12 to cover private tuition fees for eligible SEBC students pursuing science education in higher secondary classes.

schemeName: Direct Finance Scheme by NSFDC (GSCDC)
briefDescription: Under this scheme, loans are offered at a low interest rate, ranging from 1% to 5%, specifically for self-employment to scheduled caste (SC) individuals.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Scheme for Economic Empowerment of De-notified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Tribes - "Educational Empowerment" Component
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide good coaching quality for De-notified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Tribes candidates to enable them to appear in competitive examinations. Provides free coaching and stipend support to students from these marginalized communities.

schemeName: Meghalaya Cinema Theatre Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme's objective is to promote youth entrepreneurship and employment in Meghalaya by supporting cinema theatres while preserving the state’s cultural heritage.

schemeName: Divisional Tripartite Competition
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote theatrical arts and drama performances across Gujarat by organizing competitive events at divisional and state levels. Individual performers and theatre organizations receive cash prizes based on their performance rankings in state-level competitions.

schemeName: Trekking in Forest Area (Scheduled Tribes)
briefDescription: The scheme "Trekking in Forest Area (Scheduled Tribes)" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to promote love and awareness for nature among youth by organizing trekking programs in forest areas for selected Scheduled Tribes young men and women.

schemeName: SERB - POWER Research Grants
briefDescription: The aim of the ‘SERB - POWER Research Grants’ is to encourage emerging and eminent women researchers for individual-centric and competitive mode of research funding to undertake R&D activities in frontier areas of science and engineering.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Gharaunda-Group Home for Adults Scheme
briefDescription: Gharaunda (Group Home for Adults) scheme aims at setting up Gharaunda Centres or Registered Organisations (RO) for for all Adult PwDs covered under the National Trust Act with adequate and quality care service with acceptable living standards including provision of basic medical care from profession

schemeName: SERB - POWER Fellowship
briefDescription: The SERB - POWER Fellowship is designed to identify and reward excellent women researchers in Indian academic institutions and R&D laboratories, holding Ph.D. degrees in any branch of science and engineering.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemeName: Scheme for Financial Assistance to select States/UTs for Skill Development in Electronics System Design and Manufacturing (ESDM) sector
briefDescription: The scheme aims to enhance skill development capacity in the ESDM sector. Through this scheme, financial assistance and industry-relevant training are provided to students and unemployed youth.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemeName: Northeast BPO Promotion Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote the establishment of BPO and Information Technology Enabled Services units in the North-Eastern region. Through the scheme, financial assistance in the form of Viability Gap Funding is provided to eligible companies.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemeName: Scheme for ‘Skill Development in ESDM for Digital India’
briefDescription: The scheme aims to skill 3,28,000 candidates in the ESDM sector. Through this scheme, training and certification benefits are provided to students and unemployed youth.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: Merit·cum·Means Scholarships For Under Graduate Studies In Various Branches Of Agriculture And Animal Science Subjects
briefDescription: The scheme of "Merit·cum·Means Scholarships For Under Graduate Studies In Various Branches Of Agriculture And Animal Science Subjects" was introduced by ICAR with a view to attracting meritorious undergraduate students to prosecute their studies up to graduation level.

schemeName: Scheme of Internship of Students to DRDO Labs/Estts for Project Training
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide an opportunity and exposure to future graduates and postgraduates in Engineering and General Sciences to the research and development activities carried out by DRDO in state-of-the-art technology in fields relevant to the defense sector.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemeName: GEN-NEXT Support for Innovative Startups (GENESIS)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to discover, support, grow, and accelerate successful startups in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities, with a focus on inclusivity, accessibility, and affordability.

schemeName: National Geoscience Awards
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to honour individuals and teams for extraordinary achievements and outstanding contributions in the field of mineral discovery & exploration, fundamental / applied geosciences, mining and allied areas.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: National Award for Individual Excellence
briefDescription: The objective of this scheme is to recognize and encourage individuals working for the empowerment of persons with disabilities, including those covered under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016.

schemeName: Kendriya Grihmantri Dakshata Padak
briefDescription: The scheme aims to recognize excellent work, promote high professional standards, and boost morale of personnel serving in police forces, security organizations, intelligence units, and forensic science institutions across India.

schemeName: Chief Minister Small and Marginal Farmers Older Pension Scheme
briefDescription: The "Chief Minister Small and Marginal Farmers Older Pension Scheme" by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Rajasthan, aims to provide financial assistance to elderly small and marginal farmers.

schemeName: National AIDS Control Organisation (NACO) Internship Programme
briefDescription: NACO initiated the Internship Programme for young students who wish to engage with the Government. The internship programme envisages an opportunity for young students to get familiar with and understand the various dimensions of policy-making & implementation of the National AIDS Control Programme.

schemeName: Kunwar Bai Nu Mameru Yojana Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial support to ST families during the marriage of girls, providing ₹12,000 per couple as financial assistance to eligible tribal women across the state.

schemeName: Assistance for Purchase of Educational Instruments for Medical, Engineering and Technical Diploma Courses
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide annual financial assistance of ₹15,000, ₹10,000, and ₹5,000 for ST students in medical, engineering, and diploma courses, respectively.

schemeName: Advocate Graduate Stipend Gujarat
briefDescription: Under this scheme, junior lawyers receive stipends up to ₹10,000 per month and their senior advocates receive an allowance of ₹5,000 per month.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Environment,forests and climate change
schemeName: Internship Programme Of The Wildlife Crime Control Bureau (MoEFCC)
briefDescription: Internship at Wildlife Crime Control Bureau (WCCB) provides an excellent opportunity for students/researchers to familiarize themselves with various issues related to wildlife & the functioning of WCCB. The intern shall have an orientation session and will be assigned specific areas of work.

schemeName: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to reduce maternal and neonatal mortality by promoting institutional deliveries among poor pregnant women. It offers cash assistance for institutional and home deliveries, along with antenatal and postnatal care support, primarily benefiting poor pregnant women across India.

schemeName: Indo-French Postdoctoral Research Programme for Women in Science
briefDescription: This programme will provide a platform for women candidates to engage & work with Indian/French scientific communities with an aim to accelerate their talented instincts towards carrying out advanced studies and research in the frontier areas of Science & Technology.

schemeName: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Quality Upgradation / Certification: Supply of Baby Pulpers
briefDescription: The scheme "Supply of Baby Pulpers" aims to facilitate the improvement of the quality of coffee produced by growers by encouraging them to establish infrastructure for quality enhancement and obtain certification for their coffees, enabling them to earn better returns.

schemeName: Women Scientist Scheme-C
briefDescription: A scheme by MoST for female candidates with a break in their career interested in addressing societal challenges through viable S&T interventions. 1-year training is provided in Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs), patent search, know-how, drafting, filing, trademarks, trade secrets, and copyright.

schemeName: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme
briefDescription: A non-contributory pension scheme to provide social security to widows of poor family (BPL)  of the society.

schemeName: Mahila Samman Savings Certificate
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide a secure savings option with assured returns for women. Through this scheme, 7.5% interest and flexible savings benefits are provided to women and minor girls.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Petroleum and Natural Gas
schemeName: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana 2.0
briefDescription: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MOPNG), introduced the ‘"Pradhan Mantri Ujjawala Yojana 2.0" in India. The Pradhan Mantri Ujjawala Yojana (PMUY) is a scheme launched by the Government of India in 2016 to provide free LPG (liquefied petroleum gas) connections to women from below-poverty-line.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemeName: Internship Programme for Women Students / Scholars / Social Activists / Teachers
briefDescription: The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched the Internship Programme aims to harness the talent of the trainees, giving them an opportunity to work towards bringing more efficiency in the implementation of the schemes.

schemeName: VAANI (Vibrant Advocacy for Advancement and Nurturing of Indian Languages)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to create a knowledge base by conducting conferences, seminars, and workshops in twenty-two Indian languages. The scheme provides a maximum funding of ₹2,00,000/- per programme to eligible institutions, along with an honorarium of up to ₹5,000/- per session for subject experts.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemeName: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme is to provide maternity benefits to women belonging to socially and economically disadvantaged sections of society. For first child the maternity benefit of ₹5,000 is provided in two instalments under PMMVY and second child, it is ₹6,000 is to be provided subject to second child is girl.

schemeName: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Sericulture In Irrigated Area
briefDescription: The scheme provides loans to farmers for sericulture activities in irrigated areas, with a loan amount ranging from ₹49,000/- to ₹79,000/- per acre, and interest rates from 11% to 12.75%.

schemeName: National Centre For Communication Security (NCCS) Internship Scheme
briefDescription: The National Centre for Communication Security (NCCS) Internship Scheme allows young talent to be associated with the NCCS work for mutual benefit. It will enable NCCS to interact with young scholars and to get fresh ideas and study/ research support from the field of academics.

schemeName: ICMR-Junior Research Fellowship
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance and research opportunities to eligible candidates through Junior Research Fellowship for pursuing Ph.D. programmes in medical and social science fields.

schemeName: Welfare Services for Disabled Persons
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide support, rehabilitation, and empowerment to persons with disabilities through financial assistance for healthcare, education, vocational training, and assistive devices.

schemeName: DPIIT Internship Scheme
briefDescription: DPIIT has introduced an internship scheme for students pursuing Under Graduate/Post Graduate degrees or Research Scholars enrolled in recognized universities/Institutions within India or abroad. The applicants can choose the  period of their choice ranging from one/two/three months.

schemeName: Scheme For Award Of Senior/Junior Fellowships To Outstanding Persons In The Fields Of Culture
briefDescription: The scheme “Scheme for Award of Senior/Junior Fellowships to Outstanding Persons in the Fields of Culture” was launched by the Ministry of Culture, Government of India.

schemeName: Distribution of Iron Boxes at Free of Cost
briefDescription: The scheme provides complimentary iron boxes to support individuals from Backward Classes (BC), Most Backward Classes (MBC), and Denotified Communities (DNCs) engaged in washing work, aiming to improve their livelihood.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Divyangjan Kaushal Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to ensure dignified livelihood opportunities for Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) by providing industry-relevant and customized training specific to each group. The scheme provides skill development in high-growth sectors such as Information Technology, Animation, Visual Effect, etc.

schemeName: Short Term Fellowship under the Human Resource Development Programme for Health Research
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide short-term advanced training and financial support to medical and health research personnel to enhance their skills and promote research in priority health areas.

schemeName: Sagar Samman Award- Recognition of Outstanding Maritime Training Institutes
briefDescription: The scheme aims to recognize excellence in maritime education and training by providing rankings and recognition to top maritime institutes.

schemeName: Post-Matric Scholarship for the Students Belonging to Scheduled Tribe for Studies in India
briefDescription: The scheme aims to enable eligible Scheduled Tribe students to undertake quality education from the post-matriculation to the post-graduate level. It provides financial assistance covering compulsory course fees and monthly stipends for intended beneficiaries, including hostellers and day scholars.

schemeName: Welfare Services for Children
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide care, protection, and development support to vulnerable children such as orphans, street children, child labourers, and children affected by HIV/AIDS. It focuses on shelter, education, health, and rehabilitation services.

schemeName: Uttar Poorva Transformative Industrialization Scheme
briefDescription: The UNNATI (Uttar Poorva Transformative Industrialization Scheme), 2024, is a Government of India initiative for Northeastern region of India offering various incentives to the industries in both sectors Manufacturing and Service.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Education (HPBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The Financial Assistance for Education Scheme has been introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB) to provide annual educational assistance for up to two children of a beneficiary, from Class I to professional and research courses.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Dr. Ambedakar Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Post-Matric Scholarships for the Economically Backward Class (EBC) Students
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to the Economically Backward Class (EBC) students studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education. These scholarships shall be available for studies in India only.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Assistance to Voluntary Organizations Working for Welfare of OBCs
briefDescription: The "Assistance to Voluntary Organizations Working for Welfare of OBCs" scheme aims to involve the voluntary sector in improving the educational and socio-economic conditions of the target group i.e. OBCs to upgrade skills to enable them to start income-generating activities on their own.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemeName: PM Vishwakarma
briefDescription: The scheme "PM Vishwakarma " by the Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises, is a new scheme that envisages providing end-to-end holistic support to traditional artisans and craftspeople in scaling up their conventional products and services.

schemeName: AICTE – Saksham Scholarship Scheme For Specially-Abled Student (Diploma)
briefDescription: Saksham is a Scholarship Scheme by the Ministry of Education, implemented by AICTE, aimed at providing encouragement and support to specially-abled children to pursue technical education. This is an attempt to give every young specially-abled student the opportunity to study further.

schemeName: Biotechnology Career Advancement And Re-orientation (BioCARe) Programme For Women Scientists
briefDescription: Biotechnology Career Advancement and Re-orientation (BioCARe) Programme, a special scheme of the Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Ministry of Science & Technology, the government of India aims to enhance the participation of women scientists in India towards research in Biotechnology.

schemeName: Internship Program At The Indian Patent Office
briefDescription: The Internship Program by the Indian Patent Office recognizes the abundance of creative and innovative energies that flow in India, and the need to tap into and channelize these energies. The program aims to work towards the fulfilment of the objectives of the National IPR Policy.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: SAHAKAR MITRA: NCDC’s Scheme on Internship Programme
briefDescription: The programme aims at providing an opportunity to professional graduates to get experience in areas of functioning of NCDC and related aspects of cooperatives.

schemeName: Rajbhasha Gaurav Puraskar Scheme for writing original book in Hindi
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage original and translated book writing in Hindi. Through this scheme, monetary awards, certificates, and mementos are provided to Indian authors.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemeName: Visvesvaraya PhD Scheme for Electronics and IT: Post-Doctoral Fellowships (PDF)
briefDescription: This fellowship is meant for those who have a proven track record as evident from their research publications & recognitions. Support is provided for 225 Post-Doctoral Fellowships for candidates for a period of 1 year, to encourage specialized research in emerging area.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: National Agriculture Market
briefDescription: Launched on 14th April 2016, e-NAM is a pan-India electronic trading platform that links existing mandis to create a unified online market for agricultural commodities, promoting “One Nation One Market” and enabling transparent, efficient, and nationwide agri-trade.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Water Resources,River Development & Ganga Rejuvenation
schemeName: Research and Development Programme in Water Sector
briefDescription: The scheme aims to solve water sector problems through effective research and development. It provides Grants-in-Aid to academicians, universities, laboratories, government departments, and Non-Governmental Organizations to fund basic, applied, and action research projects.

beneficiaryState: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
schemeName: Integrated Dairy Development Project (IDDP)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to strengthen dairy livelihoods through Gir cow induction. Through this scheme, financial assistance and subsidies are provided to beneficiaries for cows, feed, infrastructure, and insurance.

schemeName: Chief Ministergi Artistesinggi Tengbang (CMAT) - Scheme For Award To Distinguished Artistes
briefDescription: The scheme "CMAT - Scheme for Award to Distinguished Artistes" is a scheme by the Dept. of Art & Culture, Govt. of Manipur, to encourage and felicitate the artistes (in the field of culture) belonging to different fields for their achievements such as winning National Awards namely Padma Award, etc.

schemeName: Financial Support Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to financially weak students of the University of Delhi to help them overcome financial challenges and continue their education without hardship.

schemeName: Assistance to Voluntary Organisations for Promotion of the Official Languages in the Field of Law
briefDescription: The scheme aims to propagate and develop literature in the field of law in Hindi and other regional languages. It provides financial grants to voluntary organisations for publishing legal books, commentaries, and journals.

schemeName: Innovation In Science Pursuit For Inspired Research (INSPIRE) - Faculty Fellowship
briefDescription: The "INSPIRE FACULTY FELLOWSHIP" component provides opportunities to 100 persons every year with Ph.D. qualifications in the age group of 27-32 years for 5 years to carry out research in both basic and applied science areas including engineering, medicine, agriculture, and veterinary sciences.

schemeName: NAMO Shree Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to ensure proper nutritional support to pregnant women and lactating mothers and encourage institutional deliveries. It provides financial assistance of ₹12,000/- to eligible women beneficiaries covering expenses from conception to delivery.

schemeName: National Pension Scheme For Traders And Self Employed Persons
briefDescription: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme by the Ministry of Labour and Employment. This scheme is meant for old age protection and social security of Small Scale Traders and Retailers. The beneficiary would receive a minimum assured pension of ₹ 3000/- per month after attaining the age of 60 yrs.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Environment,forests and climate change
schemeName: Ecomark Scheme of India (ECOMARK)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage the demand for environment-friendly products that cause lesser adverse impacts on the environment. It provides an Ecomark label for products meeting specified environmental criteria to eligible producers, manufacturers, importers, and exporters.

schemeName: NSAP - Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme
briefDescription: "Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme" is one of the five sub-schemes of the National Social Assistance Programme. Under IGNOAPS, citizens living Below Poverty Line and 60 years or above in age are eligible to apply. A monthly pension of ₹ 200 up to 79 years and ₹ 500 thereafter.

schemeName: Buti Foundation Award
briefDescription: The Buti Foundation Award, established in 2007 by the Buti Foundation, New Delhi, is a biennial award recognizing innovative and outstanding research in the field of Plasma Science & Technology. The award aims to honor a Scientist/Engineer under the age of 45 years for their significant contribution

schemeName: Scheme of Cash Incentive to Medal Winners in International Sports Events and their Coaches
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to reward outstanding sportspersons for their achievements at the global level. It aims to simplify eligibility, improve the award process, motivate athletes to achieve higher, and inspire young people to take up sports.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemeName: Mission Vatsalya (Child Protection Services and Child Welfare Services)
briefDescription: This scheme aims to ensure, sustain, and strengthen child protection at the family and community levels, promote family-based non-institutional care, and provide financial support to children in need.

beneficiaryState: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
schemeName: Scheme for Promotion of Affordable Rental Smart Housing (SPARSH 2.0): Government Affordable Rental Housing Development Support
briefDescription: The scheme aims to increase affordable rental housing infrastructure. Through this scheme, land identification and government housing development support are provided.

schemeName: PRL's Vikram Sarabhai Postdoctoral Fellowship (VISHWAS)
briefDescription: The Vikram Sarabhai Postdoctoral Fellowship (VISHWAS) program is an initiative by the Physical Research Laboratory (PRL) to honor the legacy of Prof. Vikram Sarabhai by supporting young doctorates to pursue groundbreaking research in fundamental science with societal impact.

schemeName: AFFDF-Financial Assistance For Treatment Of Serious Diseases To Non Pensioner Ex-Servicemen (All Ranks)/Widows
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to non-pensioner Ex-Servicemen of all ranks and widows for meeting medical expenses incurred on the treatment of approved serious diseases, including cancer, renal failure, knee replacement, and heart surgery.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: ICAR Emeritus Scientist Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to utilize the expertise of outstanding superannuated scientists of the National Agricultural Research System by supporting nationally important research, teaching, human resource development, and extension activities through honorarium and research grants.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: ICAR Emeritus Professor
briefDescription: The ICAR floated the Scheme of Emeritus Professor in order to provide an opportunity to the superannuated teachers/ Scientists of universities, colleges and institutions under ICAR-AU System.

schemeName: Ladli Beti Scheme
briefDescription: A social assistance scheme sponsored by the J&K Govt. for newborn girl children born on or after 01/04/2015. The objective is to arrest the declining female sex ratio. The scheme ensures that the girl child does not become a burden for the parent or guardian at the time of her marriage.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemeName: Self Employment Scheme For Rehabilitation Of Manual Scavengers
briefDescription: The Self Employment Scheme for Rehabilitation of Manual Scavengers (SRMS) was introduced in January 2017 by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, to assist the identified manual scavengers and their dependants in rehabilitation in alternative occupations.

schemeName: Rajasthan Chief Minister's Relief Fund - Accident Assistance
briefDescription: "Rajasthan Chief Minister's Relief Fund - Accident Assistance" is a scheme by the Chief Minister's Secretariat, Govt. of Rajasthan. In case of death due to accident, ₹ 1,00,000 is provided. In case of serious injury, assistance is up to ₹ 20,000. The assistance is "one-time full-support".

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Kanyadaan Yojana
briefDescription: Mukhyamantri Kanyadaan Yojana launched by Social Justice and empowerment in 2015 by Rajasthan for Family provides financial support to eligible families to help them cover the expenses associated with their daughters' weddings.

schemeName: Chief Minister Housing Assistance Programme
briefDescription: The scheme "Chief Minister’s Housing Assistance Programme" by the Housing Department, Government of Meghalaya, aims to provide durable housing to EWS and LIG families. Through this scheme, roofing materials and dwelling houses are provided to eligible families in the State.

schemeName: Vivah Sahayata Yojana
briefDescription: "Vivah Sahayata Yojana" is a scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹ 30,000/- (Rupees Thirty Thousand Only) rupees for the marriage of two children / female members of the family.

schemeName: National Family Benefit Scheme - Sikkim
briefDescription: The scheme “National Family Benefit Scheme (NFBS)” was launched in 2016 by the Department of Women & Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim. Under this scheme, a lump sum amount of ₹ 30,000/- as financial assistance is provided to the bereaved household in the event of the death of the "Primary breadwinner"

schemeName: Maintenance Of Pregnant Desi/Indigenous Cow/Buffalo Ration Scheme For BPL Families Belonging To Scheduled Caste (SC) Category
briefDescription: A scheme had been launched by the Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Himachal Pradesh with the aim to provides a 50% subsidy on a balanced ration for pregnant cows or buffalo during the last trimester of pregnancy.

schemeName: Krishak Durghatna Kalyan Yojana
briefDescription: The government will provide compensation to the farmers after any accident.

schemeName: KALIA Scholarship
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to extend financial assistance to the children of the beneficiaries under KALIA for pursuing Professional/Technical courses in the State of Odisha.

schemeName: Tripura Lynching/Mob Violence Victim Compensation Scheme
briefDescription: The “Tripura Lynching/Mob Violence Victim Compensation Scheme” was launched by the Home Department, Govt. of Tripura on 14th August 2018 for providing funds for the purpose of compensation to the victim or his dependents who have suffered loss or injury as a result of lynching/mob violence.

schemeName: "Immediate Relief Assistance" under "Welfare and Relief for Fishermen During Lean Seasons and Natural Calamities Scheme"
briefDescription: The scheme "Immediate Relief Assistance" under "Welfare and Relief for Fishermen During Lean Seasons and Natural Calamities Scheme" is introduced to extend financial assistance to the fishermen's families to compensate for the loss due to the missing breadwinner and to support them financially.

schemeName: Grant Of Incentive For Marriage Between Differently Abled Person And Normal Persons
briefDescription: “Grant Of Incentive For Marriage Between Differently Abled Person And Normal Persons” by the Dept of Social Welfare, Govt of Puducherry supports marriages between differently-abled individuals and those without disabilities. An incentive of ₹1,00,000/- is provided (₹20,000 as cash, and ₹80 as NSC).

schemeShortTitle: GOPINATH MUNDE SHETKARI APGHAT SURAKSHA SANUGRAH AUDHAN YOJANA
schemeName: Gopinath Munde Shetkari Apghat Suraksha Sanugrah Audhan Yojana
briefDescription: In order to give financial support to the aggrieved family members of the ill-fated farmers the State Govt. has provided accident claim cover through Gopinath Munde Shetkari Apghat Suraksha Sanugrah Audhan Yojana in the year 2023-24.

schemeName: Shubhshakti Yojana
briefDescription: The Shubshakti Yojana has been initiated by the Rajasthan government with the aim of providing financial assistance to regular construction workers, unmarried laborers, and their daughters.

schemeName: Medical Assistance (ANBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme provides medical assistance to the Building & Other Construction Workers, who are registered under the A&N Islands Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, and his/her dependents' family members during hospitalization due to accident or illness.

schemeName: Death Benefit (ANBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance to the nominees or dependents of workers engaged in Building or Other Construction Works in Andaman & Nicobar Islands in the event of their natural or accidental death.

schemeName: Shiksha Protsaahan Puraskaar yojana
briefDescription: By the Labour Department of Madhya Pradesh, a scheme (Shiksha Protsahan Puraskar Yojana) was initiated for the financial assistance to the children.

schemeName: Shree Nanaji Deshmukh Awas Yojana (GBOCWWB)
briefDescription: In urban areas, where EWS/LIG houses have been allotted by Local Self-Government Organization/Urban Development Authority & Gujarat Housing Board under Chief Minister/Prime Minister Awas Yojana, the benefit of this scheme is given once per family to construction worker registered in the board.

schemeName: Dattopant Thengadi Mratak Shramik Aarthik Sahayata Yojana
briefDescription: The Government of Uttar Pradesh initiated a scheme "Dattopant Thengadee Mrtak Shramik Aarthik Sahaayata Yojana" to the families of deceased workers.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Coaching Classes (HBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹20,000/- for coaching classes for admission of children of registered workers in professional/technical courses and ₹1,00,000/- for preparation of Main Examination on passing Preliminary Examination of UPSC/HPSSC.

schemeName: Fare for Visiting Home Town (HBOCWWB)
briefDescription: Under this scheme, a free traveling facility to visit the hometown once in a year for a family not exceeding 5 members is provided to the registered construction workers who have a minimum of two year’s regular membership of the Board.

schemeName: Financial Assistance in Marriage of Women Workers or Daughters of Workers- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹51,000/- is provided to workers employed in the industrial & commercial establishments of the state of Haryana for the marriage of their daughters and for the self-marriage of the working women workers as Kanyadaan.

schemeName: Pension for Journalists’ Family
briefDescription: The "Pension for Journalists’ Family" was launched by the Tamil Development, Religious Endowments, and Information Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to give a monthly pension to the wife of a deceased journalist pensioner.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Encouraging Worker's Children's Participation in Sports Activities- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided to the children of the workers working in the industrial and commercial establishments of the state of Haryana for participating in sports competitions so that the children of the workers can also become good sportspersons.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Working Women Workers or Wives of Male Workers for Maternity- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹10,000/- is provided to female workers and wives of male workers working in industrial and commercial establishments in the state of Haryana for the delivery of up to two children (three girls or two boys).

schemeName: Scholarship Scheme for Worker's Children- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
briefDescription: The scheme is implemented by the Haryana Labour Welfare Board, Labour Department, Government of Haryana. The objective of this scheme is to provide financial assistance to children of workers & workers employed in industrial and commercial institutions of Haryana state to continue their studies.

schemeName: Assistance for Marriage
briefDescription: The scheme "Assistance for Marriage" was launched by the Department for the Welfare of Differently Abled Persons, Government of Tamil Nadu. Under this scheme, a sum of ₹2,000/- is given to meet the marriage expenses of the differently abled person or his son or daughter.

schemeName: Assistance on Death- Haryana Silicosis Board
briefDescription: Under this scheme, in case of death of the Silicosis victim, financial assistance of ₹1,00,000/- will be payable to the widow or widower if the deceased was married, or to his/her nominee in case deceased was unmarried.

schemeName: Funeral Assistance- Haryana Silicosis Board
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹15,000/- is given to the dependent/nominee of a deceased worker, who was confirmed to have Silicosis by the Silicosis Diagnosis Board, Haryana, for performing the funerary rituals.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Nikah Yojana
briefDescription: The objective of this scheme is to provide financial assistance to the needy girls, widows, and abandoned women from destitute or poor Muslim families in the state at the time of their marriage (In case of mass marriage).

schemeName: Shagun Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
briefDescription: The "Shagun Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides financial support for the marriage of up to two daughters of registered workmen, including daughters who are registered beneficiaries.

schemeName: General Surgery Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
briefDescription: The "General Surgery Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides financial assistance for general surgery of the beneficiary and his family members.

schemeName: Cycle Scheme for the Children of Construction Workers (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
briefDescription: The "Cycle Scheme for the Children of Construction Workers'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides free cycles to the children of registered construction workers once during the period they study in 9th to 12th class in the State of Punjab.

schemeName: Urban Health Kiosk Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
briefDescription: The "Urban Health Kiosk Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that aims to provide primary health facilities to the construction workers & their families in urban health kiosks run by Punjab Health Society in slum areas.

schemeName: Mobile Lab Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
briefDescription: The "Mobile Lab Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides reimbursement of expenditure incurred by the beneficiaries and their dependents for the treatment of silicosis.

schemeName: Antarjatiye Vivah Protsahan Anudan Yojana
briefDescription: The objective of this scheme is to end the caste system in the state and encourage inter-caste marriages by providing financial assistance to couples. Under this scheme, eligible beneficiaries will receive a one-time grant of ₹1,00,000/-. This scheme is fully funded by the Government of Bihar.

schemeName: Jharkhand Mukhyamantri Maiya Samman Yojana
briefDescription: The "Jharkhand Mukhyamantri Maiya Samman Yojana" provides financial assistance to women beneficiaries. This initiative aims to enhance the financial security and well-being of women across the state.

schemeName: Kabir Anthyesthi Anudan Yojana
briefDescription: Under this scheme, a one-time grant of ₹3,000/- is given to the family/dependent of the deceased to cover funeral expenses. The grant is provided for the death of any person of any age from a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.

schemeName: Annal Gandhi Memorial Award
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance to one boy and one girl from the SC/ST community (Hindu Adi Dravidar) in each district who secure the first rank in plus 2 Public Examinations.

schemeName: Pension to the Beneficiary Building Worker Who Has Completed 60 Years
briefDescription: The Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board provides a monthly pension to workers who have completed 5 years of service as a registered beneficiary and reached 60.

schemeName: Natural Death Relief (MBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The "Natural Death Relief" scheme launched by the Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board provides financial assistance to the kith and kin of a registered building worker in the event of the worker's natural death.

schemeName: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Assistance for Education of Children
briefDescription: This scheme provides financial support to beneficiaries for their children's (maximum two) education at various levels, including higher secondary, graduation, post-graduation, and professional courses like engineering and medical studies.

schemeName: Pension Scheme (HPBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The "Pension Scheme" by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Himachal Pradesh provides registered beneficiaries with a minimum pension of ₹1,000/- per month upon reaching 60 years of age.

schemeName: Funeral Support Scheme (HPBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The "Funeral Support Scheme" under the Labour and Employment Department, Himachal Pradesh, provides ₹20,000 to the nominees or dependents of registered beneficiaries to support funeral expenses.

schemeName: Jharkhand Mukhyamantri Shramik Yojana
briefDescription: The "Mukhyamantri Shramik Yojana" scheme by the Urban Development & Housing Department, Government of Jharkhand aims to provide guaranteed employment to unskilled urban workers. It offers up to 100 days of employment in urban development projects.

schemeName: Mentally Retarded Children Benefit Scheme (HPBOCWWB)
briefDescription: In the scheme "Mentally Retarded Children Benefit" by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, an amount of ₹20,000/- per annum is provided to the registered workers to take care of their mentally retarded/disabled children.

schemeName: Family Pension (BBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme "Family Pension" by the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (BBOCWWB), Labour Resources Department, Government of Bihar, provides pension to the dependent of the deceased pensioner of the Board.

schemeName: Death Assistance Scheme (BBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The "Death Assistance" scheme provides financial assistance to the legal heir in the case of natural death or accidental death of the registered worker. If the death occurs during a disaster and a grant has already been given by Disaster Management, only ₹1,00,000/- will be payable by the Board.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Cremation (BBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme "Financial Assistance for Cremation" by the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Resources Department, Government of Bihar, provides financial assistance of ₹5,000/-to the dependents of the deceased worker for performing cremation.

schemeName: Birsa Harit Gram Yojana
briefDescription: The "Birsa Harit Gram Yojana" by the Rural Development Department, Government of Jharkhand, promotes afforestation while generating sustainable livelihoods. It supports SC, ST, and landless families through plantation activities under MGNREGA, ensuring income and environmental sustainability.

schemeName: Vishesh Yogyajan Sukhad Dampatya Jeevan Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to specially-abled individuals to support a happy married life after marriage. Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹50,000/- per couple is provided.

schemeName: Bunkar Parivar Sahayata Yojana
briefDescription: "Bunkar Parivar Sahayata Yojana" aims to provide immediate financial assistance to families of weavers in case of sudden death. The scheme benefits the families of working weavers who are members of primary weaver cooperative societies by providing financial support of ₹20,000/-.

schemeName: Incentive for Inter-Caste Marriage Scheme
briefDescription: "Incentive For Inter Caste Marriage" aims to promote inter-caste marriages between SC and non-SC communities to combat casteism. The scheme provides a financial incentive of ₹50,000/- to eligible couples, with either spouse being a permanent resident of NCT of Delhi.

schemeName: Mukhyamantri Nishulk Gas Refill Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to support poor families by reducing their household fuel expenses. Under this scheme, Antyodaya (pink card) ration card holders in the state are entitled to receive three free gas cylinder refills every year.

schemeName: Solar Home System (Manohar Jyoti)
briefDescription: The "Solar Home System (Manohar Jyoti)" by the Renewable Energy Department, Government of Haryana, aims to promote renewable energy adoption and ensure sustainable power access through solar solutions. Through this scheme, a subsidized solar home lighting system is provided to families.

schemeName: Solar Inverter Charger
briefDescription: The "Solar Inverter Charger" by the Renewable Energy Department, Government of Haryana, aims to promote the use of renewable energy and reduce electricity dependence by providing a subsidy of ₹6,000/- or ₹10,000/- for solar inverter charger systems to eligible households.

schemeName: National Family Benefit Scheme - Gujarat
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide social security to Below Poverty Line (BPL) households. Under the scheme, a one-time lump sum assistance of ₹20,000 is provided to the widow or unmarried daughter of the deceased, if the primary breadwinner, aged between 18 and 59 years, passes away.

schemeName: Rental Housing Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide rental housing to individuals and families from lower and middle-income groups, address housing shortages, prevent unauthorized settlements, and promote orderly urban development. It benefits Grade Third and Grade Fourth employees and eligible non-government individuals.

schemeName: Shahidvir Kinariwaala Student Security Group Insurance Scheme
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial protection in case of accidental death or permanent disability to students or their guardians/heirs to help reduce the burden during unforeseen tragedies. The scheme covers students at Government and Non-Government Colleges, Gram Vidyapeeth GBTC colleges, etc.

schemeName: Antyodaya Gruha Yojana: Incentive to Beneficiaries
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide time-bound completion incentives for rural housing construction. Through this scheme, incentives are provided to eligible beneficiaries who complete house construction within 4 and 6 months, respectively.

schemeName: Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Medical Assistance
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide healthcare and medical assistance to registered drivers, conductors, and their families. Medical benefits are delivered through existing state health schemes.

schemeName: Antyodaya Gruha Yojana: Construction of New House
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to eligible rural households for the construction of a new pucca house.

schemeName: Deendayal Antyodaya Upchar Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide eligible BPL families with free treatment cards by issuing health access cards within a fixed time limit.

schemeName: Zupda Vijlikaran Yojana
briefDescription: Under this scheme, free household electricity connections are given to the BPL /Lower income group households across the state.

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemeName: Rainfed Area Development
briefDescription: Rainfed Area Development (RAD) scheme was launched in 2014-15 to mainstream development of rainfed areas in a sustainable manner. It adopts an area-based approach, focuses on Integrated Farming System (IFS) for enhancing productivity and minimizing risks associated with climatic variability.

schemeName: Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme for Middle Income Group
briefDescription: Under Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme, beneficiaries of Middle-Income Group (MIG) can seek housing loans from Banks, Housing Finance Companies and other such institutions for acquisition/ construction of house (including re-purchase).

schemeName: Pradhan Mantri Janjatiya Vikas Mission (PMJVM)
briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote livelihood generation and income enhancement of tribal communities through value addition, processing, and marketing of tribal products.

schemeName: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana - Gramin
briefDescription: Launch on 1st April 2016, Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana Gramin (PMAY-G) is centre's flagship mission by the Ministry of Rural Development (MoRD), implemented by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA). PMAY-G addresses the rural housing shortage and bridges the housing deficit in rural areas

nodalMinistryName: Ministry Of Petroleum and Natural Gas
schemeName: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana
briefDescription: A scheme by Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas for Providing free LPG connections to women belonging to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) households.

schemeName: Tripura Scheme For Incentive To Girl Child
briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide an incentive to girl children. Through this scheme, an incentive is offered to the mother of the girl child or her legal guardian on a monthly basis.

schemeName: Janta Juth Accidental Insurance Scheme
briefDescription: "Janta Juth" is a Farmer's Accidental Insurance Scheme by the Govt. of Gujarat to provide economic assistance of upto ₹ 2,00,000 to Farmers in case of Death or Disability. The objective is to assist the successor of the farmer in case of death or disability due to an accident.

schemeName: Chief Minister's COVID-19 Affected Livelihood Support Scheme
briefDescription: The guidelines of the "Chief Minister's COVID-19 Affected Livelihood Support Scheme" were approved by the Dept. of Social Welfare, Govt. of Manipur. The COVID-19 Pandemic and the resulting economic dislocations have severely affected the livelihood of a large percentage of the population.

schemeName: Parivaar Pension Yojana (JBOCWWB)
briefDescription: "Parivaar Pension Yojana (JBOCWWB)" is a scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. The scheme provides 50% of the pension amount or a maximum of ₹ 500/- to the family members of the deceased pensioner.

schemeName: Mamta Taruni Abhiyan
briefDescription: "Mamta Taruni Abhiyan" was a program launched in Gujarat, India, aimed at providing healthcare facilities to adolescent girls and pregnant women in rural areas. The program was launched by the Gujarat state government in 2010.

schemeName: Silpi Pension Scheme - Assam
briefDescription: Silpi Pension Scheme is a Welfare Scheme provided by the Government of Assam to support the Artists of Assam. Those artists and families who have made outstanding contributions in the field of Art and Culture of Assam will be given financial aid and Pension.

schemeName: NIJASHAREE HOUSING SCHEME
briefDescription: The scheme “NIJASHAREE HOUSING SCHEME” is an initiative by the Govt. of West Bengal which envisages the construction of multi-storied Lower Income Group (LIG)/Middle Income Group (MIG) flats on the public lands belonging to the State Government, local bodies and parastatals.

schemeName: Death Benefit Scheme (APB&OCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme “Death Benefit Scheme” was started by the Department of Labour and Employment, Govt. of Arunachal Pradesh for registered workers of the Arunachal Pradesh Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB).

schemeName: Funeral Financial Assistant (APB&OCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme “Funeral Financial Assistant” was started by the Department of Labour and Employment, Govt. of Arunachal Pradesh for the registered workers of Arunachal Pradesh Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB).

schemeName: Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana
briefDescription: Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana (BSKY) has been launched by the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Odisha as a path-breaking program to provide universal health coverage, with special emphasis on the health protection of vulnerable families and women.

schemeName: Death Assistance For Registered Worker (HBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme “Death Assistance for Registered Worker (HBOCWWB)” was launched by the Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HBOCWWB), Labour Department Haryana.

schemeName: Self-Employment Scheme For Dependents Of Prisoners
briefDescription: The scheme “Self-Employment Scheme for Dependents of Prisoners” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

schemeName: Relief and Rehabilitation Assistance to Sri Lankan Refugees in the Refugee Camps
briefDescription: The scheme provides relief materials, which among other things include Feeding Charges, Subsidized Rice, Clothing Materials, Utensils, Transportation Charges (on arrival), Porterage and Transport (on repatriation), Cash Doles, Infrastructure Facilities (at camps), Electricity, Drinking Water, etc.

schemeName: Kalyana Lakshmi Pathakam
briefDescription: The scheme is designed to support economically disadvantaged families belonging to the SC/ST and minority communities by providing one-time financial assistance for the marriage of their daughters.

schemeName: Lean Season Assistance under "Welfare and Relief for Fishermen During Lean Seasons and Natural Calamities Scheme"
briefDescription: The scheme "Lean Season Assistance" under "Welfare and Relief for Fishermen During Lean Seasons and Natural Calamities Scheme" is introduced with the objective of assisting fishermen families who are deprived of income during natural calamities and fishing ban periods.

schemeName: Gopabandhu Sambadika Swasthya Bima Yojana
briefDescription: The “Gopabandhu Sambadika Swasthya Bima Yojana” was launched by the Information & Public Relations Department, Govt. of Odisha. It is a health insurance scheme that covers the medical expenses of the journalists and their eligible family members.

schemeName: Grant of Incentive for Marriage Between Differently Abled Person
briefDescription: Through this scheme, an incentive of ₹2,00,000/- is provided, comprising ₹40,000 in cash and ₹1,60,000/- in the form of a National Savings Certificate (NSC), for the marriage between differently-abled individuals. The selection is periodical and depends on the availability of funds.

schemeName: Financial Assistance to Scheduled Castes for Funeral Rites
briefDescription: “Financial Assistance to Scheduled Castes for Funeral Rites” was introduced by the Adi Dravidar Welfare Department, Government of Puducherry. The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to the Scheduled Caste family for performing funeral rites of their deceased family member.

schemeName: Tea Development Scheme
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to continue the experimental plantation of tea and to find the possibility of growing tea economically as well as on a commercial scale.

schemeName: Funeral Assistance (ANBOCWWB)
briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance to the nominees or dependents of deceased workers who were registered under the A&N Islands Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, towards their funeral expenses.

schemeName: Prasuti Sahayata Yojana
briefDescription: The Rajasthan Prasuti Sahayata Yojana is a scheme launched by the Government of Rajasthan to provide financial assistance to pregnant women from registered construction worker families in the state

schemeName: Marriage Assistance (ANBOCWWB)
briefDescription: Under this scheme, registered construction workers will receive a financial allowance to cover either their own marriage expenses or those of their two dependent children.

schemeName: Khiladi Protsahan Yojana
briefDescription: In 2017, the Madhya Pradesh Labour Department initiated the Khilaadee Protsaahan Yojana to provide financial assistance to players.

schemeName: Marriage Assistance Scheme (PBAOCWWB)
briefDescription: "Marriage Assistance" is a Welfare Scheme by the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. In this scheme, financial assistance of ₹7,000/- is provided to the registered construction workers of the Board for the marriage of their children. The benefits are credited via DBT.

schemeName: Satyvadi Raja Harishchandra Marnotar Sahay Yojana
briefDescription: The scheme was launched in the year 2000 to provide financial assistance to scheduled caste families at the time of the death of a family member to cover funeral expenses, due to their poor economic conditions.

schemeName: Marriage Assistance - Folk Artist
briefDescription: The scheme "Marriage Assistance" was launched by the Art and Culture Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. This scheme provides financial assistance for the marriage of registered folk artists, or their son or daughter. The assistance aims to support the cultural community by helping with marriage.

schemeName: Financial Assistance to Workers with a Visually, Physically or Mentally Disabled Child- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided to the disabled (blind, mentally retarded, mute and deaf) children of registered workers employed in industrial and commercial establishments in the state of Haryana.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Encouraging Worker's Children's Participation in Cultural Activities- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided to the children of the workers working in the industrial and commercial establishments of the state of Haryana for participating in cultural competitions so that the children of the workers can also become good artists.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Artificial Limbs to Workers and their Dependents- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided for artificial limbs upto rates fixed by Saket Hospital, Chandimandir(Panchkula). This assistance is available to workers employed in industrial &commercial units in Haryana and their dependents, who become disabled due to the loss of vital organs.

schemeName: Mukhya Mantri Sharmik Surksha Yojana- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹5,00,000/- will be provided to the widows/dependents of workers employed in industrial and commercial institutions in Haryana, in the event of the worker's death while working at the workplace.

schemeName: Scheme of Death Benefit for Weavers & Artisans of West Bengal
briefDescription: "Scheme of Death Benefit for Weavers & Artisans of West Bengal" is a social welfare scheme implemented by the Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises and Textiles, Government of West Bengal aims to provide financial assistance to the family of the deceased weaver /artisan.

schemeName: Financial Assistance for Coaching- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
briefDescription: The objective of the scheme to provide financial assistance up to ₹20,000/- for coaching of entrance examinations for professional courses and ₹1,00,000/- to the children of workers for preparing for the main examination on passing the preliminary examination of UPSC and HPSC.

schemeName: Karma Sathi Prakalpa
briefDescription: The scheme aims to help young entrepreneurs in the state establish new manufacturing enterprises and small businesses, including those offering services and trading. The scheme offers soft loans and subsidies for new income-generating projects.

schemeName: Incentives for Meritorious Children (Students) of Workers (10th and 12th Class) (HBOCWWB)
briefDescription: Under this scheme, the scholarship (prize money) in the form of fixed deposit will be provided to meritorious children of construction workers who have achieved academic excellence in Class X/XII examination.

schemeName: Family Pension- Haryana Silicosis Board
briefDescription: Under this scheme, after the death of the worker due to Silicosis, financial assistance of ₹3,500/- per month is provided to the spouse of the worker until he/she remains alive. In the case of an unmarried worker, the financial assistance is provided to his/her living parents.

schemeName: Relief to the Unregistered Workers
briefDescription: The "Relief to the Unregistered Workers" scheme was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F Dept.
